blocks:
Ayahs

صٓ ۚ وَٱلْقُرْءَانِ ذِى ٱلذِّكْرِ (۱)
Suad. By the Quran bearing the Reminder, (1)
بَلِ ٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا۟ فِى عِزَّةٍ وَشِقَاقٍ (۲)
the faithless indeed dwell in conceit and defiance. (2)
كَمْ أَهْلَكْنَا مِن قَبْلِهِم مِّن قَرْنٍ فَنَادَوا۟ وَّلَاتَ حِينَ مَنَاصٍ (۳)
How many a generation We have destroyed before them! They cried out [for help], but gone was the time for escape. (3)

Key Points

The word 'Izzah (عزّة) and 'Aziz (عزیز) means invulnerability or impenetrability. Solidity and invulnerability are sometimes appropriate, such as in "ان العزّة للّه و لرسوله..." (Indeed, all might belongs to Allah and His Messenger...), and sometimes inappropriate, meaning stubbornness, obstinacy, and rigidity. Sometimes the source of 'Izzah is knowledge, power, and perfections, such as the might mentioned for Allah, the Messenger, and the believers, but sometimes the source of 'Izzah is arrogance, pride, fanaticism, and conceit, like the verse under discussion: "بل الّذین كفروا فى عزّة و شقاق" (Nay, those who disbelieve are in pride and schism). The word Shiqaq (شقاق) means severe disagreement and opposition that leads to separation. Qarn (قرن) refers to people who live concurrently and in the same era. The word Lāta (لات) is composed of two letters: Lā (لا), which means negation, and the letter tā' (ت), which is for emphasis and exaggeration, similar to the letter tā' in 'Allāmah. Lāta is used to negate time, meaning: the time is not the time for such an action. The word Manās (مناص) is derived from naws (نوص), meaning refuge.(1) The letter Sād (ص) is one of the disjointed letters (Huroof Muqatta'ah), and we read in a tradition that these letters are among the ambiguous verses (Mutashābihāt) whose knowledge Allah has reserved exclusively for Himself.(2) The intent of the disbelievers' cry in "نادوا" (they cried out) is their declaration of regret for their past deeds, which will no longer benefit them. Just as in Ayah 14 of Surah Al-Anbiya, their speech is expressed thus, where they say: "یا ویلنا انّا كنّا ظالمین" (Woe to us! Indeed, we were wrongdoers). The Quran is a reminder (Dhikr). "ذى الذكر" (possessor of the Reminder) is a blessed reminder. "هذا ذكر مبارك"(3) It is a reminder for all the worlds. "ذكر للعالمین"(4) It is accessible to everyone. "لقد یسّرنا القرآن للذّكر"(5)

1) Tafsir Namuneh. 2) Tafsir Rāhnamā. 3) Anbiya, 50. 4) Sād, 87. 5) Qamar, 17.

Messages

1- Allah's oath by the Quran is a sign of the greatness of this Book and the truthfulness of the Prophet. "و القرآن"

2- The Quran awakens dormant natures and reminds people of forgotten knowledge. "ذى الذّكر"

3- Although many different and varied topics are mentioned in the Quran, the main theme in all of them is the guidance of people and reminding them. "ذى الذّكر"

4- The greatness of the Quran lies in its being the Reminder (Dhikr). "القرآن ذى الذّكر" Just as the value of a scholar lies in his not being heedless. "فسئلوا اهل الذكر"(6) and He did not say: "فاسئلوا اهل العلم"

5- Man has free will and is free in choosing his path. The Quran was revealed to remind and guide man, yet some choose the path of disbelief. "ذى الذكر بل الّذین كفروا"

6- The disbelief of the disbelievers stems from their obstinacy and rigidity, not from the Quran's failure to clearly state the truth. "بل الّذین كفروا فى عزّة و شقاق"

7- We should learn lessons from historical events. "و كم اهلكنا"

8- All pride will eventually be broken and turn into wailing and crying out. "فنادوا"

9- There is no refuge against Allah's wrath. "و لات حین مناص"

10- Repentance and wailing are effective before the occurrence of wrath. "و لات حین مناص" 6) Nahl, 43 and Anbiya, 7.

Explanations

Portrait of Surah Sad

This Surah was revealed in Mecca and has eighty-eight verses. The name of this Surah is taken from its first verse and is one of the Quranic disjointed letters (Huroof Muqatta'ah). The verses of this Surah, like Surah As-Saffat, express the continuity of the prophetic mission throughout history and the confrontation of polytheists and disbelievers with the belief in Monotheism (Tawhid) and the Resurrection (Ma'ad). In Surah As-Saffat, the names of Prophets such as Noah, Abraham, Lot, Moses, Aaron, Elijah, and Jonah were mentioned. This Surah focuses more on the history of the lives of Prophets David, Solomon, and Job علیهم السلام. The final section of the Surah describes the story of the creation of man, the prostration of the angels to Adam, and the disobedience of Satan, so that believers may realize the inherent dignity of man before Allah and avoid following Satan.

Ayah

وَعَجِبُوٓا۟ أَن جَآءَهُم مُّنذِرٌ مِّنْهُمْ ۖ وَقَالَ ٱلْكَـٰفِرُونَ هَـٰذَا سَـٰحِرٌ كَذَّابٌ (٤)
They consider it odd that there should come to them a warner from among themselves, and the faithless say, ‘This is a magician, a mendacious liar.’ (4)

Messages

1- Opponents consider the Prophet's point of strength to be his point of weakness. (Being human/from the people is a point of strength, but the disbelievers were astonished by this very fact). «عجبوا... منذر منهم»

2- The most important duty of the prophets is warning (admonition). «منذر منهم»

3- The Prophet must be of the same kind as the people so that he shares the same needs and feelings and can be a role model and leader for them. «منهم»

4- Undue arrogance and obstinacy cause a person to deny anything that does not conform to his desires. «فى عزة و شقاق و قال الكافرون...»

5- Instead of following logic, the disbelievers resorted to slander (accusations). «ساحر كذّاب»

Ayahs

أَجَعَلَ ٱلْـَٔالِهَةَ إِلَـٰهًا وَٰحِدًا ۖ إِنَّ هَـٰذَا لَشَىْءٌ عُجَابٌ (٥)
‘Has he reduced the gods to one god? This is indeed an odd thing!’ (5)
وَٱنطَلَقَ ٱلْمَلَأُ مِنْهُمْ أَنِ ٱمْشُوا۟ وَٱصْبِرُوا۟ عَلَىٰٓ ءَالِهَتِكُمْ ۖ إِنَّ هَـٰذَا لَشَىْءٌ يُرَادُ (٦)
Their elite go about [urging others]: ‘Go and stand by your gods! This is indeed the desirable thing [to do]. (6)
مَا سَمِعْنَا بِهَـٰذَا فِى ٱلْمِلَّةِ ٱلْـَٔاخِرَةِ إِنْ هَـٰذَآ إِلَّا ٱخْتِلَـٰقٌ (۷)
We did not hear of this in the latter-day creed. This is nothing but a fabrication. (7)

Key Points

‘Ujāb’ refers to something extremely strange. ‘Inṭilāq’ means going away accompanied by separation from an individual. ‘Ikhtilāq’ means the fabrication of something that has no precedent, meaning they have woven, processed, and created these things themselves. ‘Mala’ refers to a group whose name and title are prominent and eye-catching. What the disbelievers meant by ‘Millat al-Ākhirah’ (the religion of the latter people) is the religion of Prophet Christ, which was the last heavenly religion before Islam, because they believed in the Trinity. It is possible that the meaning of «انّ هذا لشى ء یراد» (Indeed, this is a thing desired) is that this call to monotheism is the very lordship and mastery that the Prophet desires and intends. If the system of idolatry is abolished, the leader of the monotheists will be the Prophet, whose will is this. He, by calling to monotheism, is thinking of gaining mastery over you.(7)

7) Tafsir al-Mizan.

Messages

1- The Prophet’s first action is the negation of false deities and the affirmation of the One God. «جعل الالهة الهاً واحداً»

2- The disbelievers were troubled regarding monotheism, «أجعل الالهة الهاً واحداً», regarding prophethood, «عجبوا ان جاءهم منذر منهم» (the previous Ayah), and regarding the Resurrection. «ء انّا لمبعوثون»(8)

3- For most people, abandoning long-held beliefs and paying attention to new ideas is difficult and astonishing. «أجعل - شى ء عجاب»

4- Sometimes falsehood gains such prominence and power that the word of truth becomes strange and unacceptable. «هذا لشى ء عجاب»

5- The leaders of disbelief, in order to make their words influential, first deviate themselves and then instruct others to deviate. «انطلق الملأ... ان امشوا»

6- Among the disbelievers, a group acts as the cause of deviation and obstinacy for others. «وانطلق الملا منهم ان امشوا واصبروا»

7- Let us recognize the disbelievers by their words and actions: A) Spreading doubt and seeking discord. «اجعل الالهة... شى ء عجاب - عجبوا» B) Keeping people away from gatherings of truth. «امشوا» C) Instead of inviting to contemplation, inviting to fanaticism and resistance. «اصبروا» D) Relying on the tradition of ancestors or proposing the tradition of others against Islam. «ما سمعنا بهذا»

8- The disbelievers exploit the inclinations of others when inviting them. «اصبروا على الهتكم»

9- Not every type of steadfastness and patience is praiseworthy. «اصبروا على الهتكم» Just as not every type of innovation is condemned such that it is said: «هذا... اختلاق»

10- Prophets dedicate patience in the way of Allah. «و لربّك فاصبر» And disbelievers dedicate patience in the way of idols. «اصبروا على آلهتكم»

11- The leaders of disbelief advertise and promote steadfastness upon deviation. «انّ هذا لشى ء یراد» 8) Isra, 49.

Ayahs

أَءُنزِلَ عَلَيْهِ ٱلذِّكْرُ مِنۢ بَيْنِنَا ۚ بَلْ هُمْ فِى شَكٍّ مِّن ذِكْرِى ۖ بَل لَّمَّا يَذُوقُوا۟ عَذَابِ (۸)
Has the Reminder been sent down to him out of [all of] us?’ Indeed, they are in doubt concerning My Reminder. Indeed, they have not yet tasted My punishment. (8)
أَمْ عِندَهُمْ خَزَآئِنُ رَحْمَةِ رَبِّكَ ٱلْعَزِيزِ ٱلْوَهَّابِ (۹)
Do they possess the treasuries of the mercy of your Lord, the All-mighty, the All-munificent? (9)
أَمْ لَهُم مُّلْكُ ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ وَٱلْأَرْضِ وَمَا بَيْنَهُمَا ۖ فَلْيَرْتَقُوا۟ فِى ٱلْأَسْبَـٰبِ (۱٠)
Do they own the kingdom of the heavens and the earth and whatever is between them? [If so,] let them ascend [to the higher spheres] by the means [of ascension]. (10)
جُندٌ مَّا هُنَالِكَ مَهْزُومٌ مِّنَ ٱلْأَحْزَابِ (۱۱)
[They are but] a host routed out there of the factions. (11)

Key Points

One of the names of the Noble Qur'an is Dhikr (Admonition/Remembrance). At the beginning of this Surah, the Qur'an was described as Dhikr: «القرآن ذى الذّكر» (The Qur'an, full of Admonition). In this Ayah (verse) too, the revelation of the Qur'an upon the Prophet is counted as the revelation of Dhikr: «انزل علیه الذّكر» (The Admonition was sent down upon him). In Ayah 48, we also read: «هذا ذكر مبارك» (This is a blessed Admonition). At the end of the Surah, it is also stated: «ان هو الاّ ذكر للعالمین» (It is nothing but an Admonition to the worlds).

Doubt (shakk) is of two types: natural and deliberate. In natural doubt, a person seeks to understand the truth but has not yet reached certainty (‘ilm). This doubt is a positive matter and a necessity of human thought. However, sometimes a person knows something but pretends to doubt it, feigning ignorance, and casts doubt upon others so that the truth is not revealed.(9) The type of doubt meant by the Qur'an when it states: «بل هم فى شك من ذكرى» (Nay, but they are in doubt concerning My Admonition) is the second type. The particle mā in «جندٌ ما» (a host) is for belittlement, meaning an insignificant and small army.

9) Tafsir al-Mizan.

Messages

1- The doubt of some disbelievers regarding the prophethood of the Prophet of Islam stems from doubt concerning the very possibility of revelation descending. «ءانزل علیه الذكر... بل هم فى شك من ذكرى»

2- Some people who find excuses regarding the rulings of the religion, in reality, do not accept the principle of the religion itself. «ءانزل علیه الذكر من بیننا بل هم فى شك...»

3- The root of some denials is the barrier of contemporaneity and envy. (Why did he become the Prophet and not us?) «ءانزل علیه الذكر من بیننا»

4- Those who belittle the divine leader and school of thought must themselves be belittled. In response to those who say: «ءانزل علیه الذكر من بیننا» (Has the Admonition been sent down upon him from among us?), asking how he became the Prophet, the Qur'an states: Who are you? Are the treasures of Allah’s Mercy in your hands, or is the dominion of the heavens in your hands? You are nothing more than a defeated group. «ام عندهم... ام لهم...»

5- Doubt, if natural, does not entail wrath or punishment, unless one could have removed it and attained certainty but failed to do so. However, if the doubt arises from arrogance, belittlement, and the weakening of others, it is followed by threat, wrath, and punishment. «بل هم فى شك من ذكرى بل لمّا یذوقوا عذاب»

6- Disbelievers will not believe until they are punished and see the Fire with their own eyes! (Because they are sensualists and only accept what they see.)

7- The sending of Prophets for the guidance of people is a manifestation of Allah’s Mercy, Might, and Generosity. «ام عندهم خزائن رحمة ربّك العزیز الوهّاب»

8- Allah is Generous (al-Wahhāb) regarding the nurturing of people, but He is impenetrable (al-‘Azīz) concerning the unreasonable and illegitimate desires of people.

9- The selection of the leader and the law must be in the hands of the God Who holds the system of existence, «ملك السموات والارض» (the dominion of the heavens and the earth), Who controls the system of human nurturing, «ربّك» (your Lord), and Whose Mercy is infinite. «خزائن رحمة» (Therefore, people have no right to say why he became the Prophet from among us and not someone else).

10- The higher realm is the means for the administration of the lower realm. «فلیرتقوا فى الاسباب»

11- Although the enemies have armies and organizations, in the face of the truth, they are neither numerous, «جندٌ ما» (a host), nor powerful, «مهزوم» (defeated), nor is their party or group unique. «من احزاب» (from the confederates).

12- Non-divine parties are doomed to defeat and extinction. «مهزوم من الاحزاب»

13- Allah informs about the unseen and the future of the disbelievers. (This very Mecca, which is the site of such objections to the Prophet’s prophethood, will one day witness their defeat during the Conquest of Mecca.) «مهزوم من الاحزاب»

Ayahs

كَذَّبَتْ قَبْلَهُمْ قَوْمُ نُوحٍ وَعَادٌ وَفِرْعَوْنُ ذُو ٱلْأَوْتَادِ (۱۲)
Before them Noah’s people impugned [their apostle] and [so did the people of] ‘Ad, and Pharaoh, the Impaler [of his victims], (12)
وَثَمُودُ وَقَوْمُ لُوطٍ وَأَصْحَـٰبُ لْـَٔيْكَةِ ۚ أُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ ٱلْأَحْزَابُ (۱۳)
and Thamud, and the people of Lot, and the inhabitants of Aykah: those were the factions. (13)
إِن كُلٌّ إِلَّا كَذَّبَ ٱلرُّسُلَ فَحَقَّ عِقَابِ (۱٤)
There was not any one but such as impugned the apostles; so My retribution became due [against them]. (14)
وَمَا يَنظُرُ هَـٰٓؤُلَآءِ إِلَّا صَيْحَةً وَٰحِدَةً مَّا لَهَا مِن فَوَاقٍ (۱٥)
These [too] do not await but a single Cry, which will not grant [them] any respite. (15)
وَقَالُوا۟ رَبَّنَا عَجِّل لَّنَا قِطَّنَا قَبْلَ يَوْمِ ٱلْحِسَابِ (۱٦)
They say, ‘Our Lord! Hasten on for us our share before the Day of Reckoning.’ (16)

Key Points

“Dhul-Awtād” (Owner of the Stakes/Pegs) is a metaphor for the consolidation of power. “Fawāq” means return (or recovery). It also refers to the interval between milkings of an animal, allowing the milk to return to the udder. “Ayka” means tree, and “Aṣḥāb al-Ayka” (Companions of the Wood) means people who lived in a land full of water and trees. In these few verses, the grim fate of the nations of six prophets preceding Islam is mentioned, so that the disbelievers of the time of the Prophet of Islam may take heed, and so that the Prophet and the believers may know that the denial of prophets has a long history and is nothing new. The people of Noah were drowned in water. «فاخذهم الطوفان» (10) Allah afflicted them with a wave-making flood in the sea. The people of 'Ād, who denied Prophet Hūd, were destroyed by a fierce gale. «فاهلكوا بریح صَرصر عاتیه» (11) They were annihilated by a loud, cold, and rebellious wind. The people of Pharaoh were destroyed in the waves of the Nile. «اغرقنا آل فرعون» (12) We drowned the people of Pharaoh. The people of Thamūd, who denied Prophet Ṣāliḥ, were annihilated by a heavenly blast. «انا ارسلنا علیهم صیحة واحدة فكانوا كهشیم المحتظر» (13) We destroyed them with a blast such that they became like the crumbled thorns and stubble found in an animal pen. The people of Lūṭ (Lot) were destroyed by an earthquake and heavenly stones. «انا ارسلنا علیهم حاصبا» (14) We destroyed them by sending stones upon them. The Companions of the Wood (Aṣḥāb al-Ayka), who denied Prophet Shu'ayb, were destroyed by a thunderbolt. «فانتقمنا منهم» (15) We took vengeance upon them.

10) 'Ankabūt, 14. 11) Ḥāqqah, 6. 12) Baqarah, 50. 13) Qamar, 31. 14) Qamar, 54. 15) Ḥijr, 79

Messages

1- Narrating the instructive history of past generations is a clear example of the Qur’an being a Reminder (Dhikr), as we read in the first verse: «والقرآن ذى الذّكر», «كذّبت قبلهم قوم نوح...»

2- The tyrants of the age resort to persecution and torture when they fail against logic. «فرعون ذوالاوتاد»

3- The people of 'Ād, Pharaoh, Noah, Thamūd, and Lūṭ were organized and unparalleled groups. «اولئك الاحزاب» (The word “Ḥizb” refers to an organized, cohesive, and aligned group, and the word «اولئك», which implies a kind of exclusivity, indicates that these were unparalleled nations.)

4- Denying one prophet is equivalent to denying all prophets. «ان كلّ الا كذّب الرسل»

5- The blast (Ṣayḥah) and the thunderbolt (Ṣā‘iqah) are among the worldly punishments of Allah. «صیحة واحدة»

6- If the punishment comes, the disbelievers will not be granted permission to return (or recover). «ما لها من فواق»

7- Denying the prophets has been the constant tradition of the adversaries throughout history. «ان كل الا كذب الرسل»

8- The disbelievers treat divine threats with mockery and ridicule. «عجّل لنا قطّنا»

9- Arrogance and obstinacy compel man to rush toward danger. «عجّل لنا قطّنا»

10- The Day of Resurrection is the Day of Reckoning. «یوم الحساب»

Ayahs

ٱصْبِرْ عَلَىٰ مَا يَقُولُونَ وَٱذْكُرْ عَبْدَنَا دَاوُۥدَ ذَا ٱلْأَيْدِ ۖ إِنَّهُۥٓ أَوَّابٌ (۱۷)
Be patient over what they say, and remember Our servant, David, [the man] of strength. Indeed he was a penitent [soul]. (17)
إِنَّا سَخَّرْنَا ٱلْجِبَالَ مَعَهُۥ يُسَبِّحْنَ بِٱلْعَشِىِّ وَٱلْإِشْرَاقِ (۱۸)
We disposed the mountains to glorify [Allah] with him at evening and dawn, (18)
وَٱلطَّيْرَ مَحْشُورَةً ۖ كُلٌّ لَّهُۥٓ أَوَّابٌ (۱۹)
and the birds [as well], mustered [in flocks]; all echoing him [in a chorus]. (19)
وَشَدَدْنَا مُلْكَهُۥ وَءَاتَيْنَـٰهُ ٱلْحِكْمَةَ وَفَصْلَ ٱلْخِطَابِ (۲٠)
We consolidated his kingdom and gave him wisdom and conclusive speech. (20)

Key Points

This Surah refers to the history of nine Prophets, the biographies of three of whom are discussed in detail and six in brief. The first is Prophet David (Dawud), who is praised for ten perfections. In these verses, ten perfections are presented for Prophet David: A) A model of patience for the Prophet of Islam صلى الله علیه وآله. «و اصبر على ما یقولون واذكر عبدنا داود» B) Servitude to Allah. «عبدنا» C) Returning to God and continuous repentance (inabah). «انّه اوّاب» D) Possessing strength. «انّا سخّرنا الجبال معه» E) The subjugation of the mountains and their harmony with him. «یسبّحن معه» F) The gathering of the birds before him. «و الطیر محشورة» G) Their harmony with him in repentance. «كلّ له اوّاب» H) Sovereignty and governance. «شددنا ملكه» I) Divine wisdom. «آتیناه الحكمة» J) Righteous judgment and settling disputes. «فصل الخطاب»

In the Qur'an, birds are mentioned in several verses, including: A) The story of Prophet Abraham and the revival of four birds. «فخذ اربعة من الطیر»(16) where birds become a means of understanding Monotheism (Tawhid) and the Resurrection (Ma'ad). B) One of the miracles of Prophet Jesus was creating a statue in the shape of a bird, which came to life when the Prophet breathed into it. «انّى اخلق لكم من الطین كهیئة الطیر فانفخ فیه فیكون طیراً»(17) C) In this verse, the birds harmonize with David. «والطیر محشورة» D) The hoopoe, which brought news to Solomon and delivered a letter. «اذهب بكتابى هذا فالقه الیهم»(18) E) Birds possess logic, and the Prophets knew it. «علّمنا منطق الطیر»(19) F) Birds that became the means of destroying enemies. «و ارسل علیهم طیراً ابابیل»(20) G) Birds possess conscious glorification (tasbih) and prayer (salat). «كلّ قد علم صلاته و تسبیحه»(21)

The meaning of wisdom (hikmah) is firm, certain, and undeniable knowledge, unlike 'ilm (general knowledge), which is always subject to error and doubt.

16) Baqarah, 260. 17) Ma'idah, 110. 18) Naml, 28. 19) Naml, 16. 20) Fil, 3. 21) Nur, 41.

Messages

1- The prerequisite for leadership is broad-mindedness and patience in the face of the bitter words of others. «اِصبر على ما یقولون»

2- Remembering the history of those who came before is a factor in enduring hardships and difficulties. «و اِصبر... واذكر»

3- Mockery and negative propaganda are the constant work of opponents. «اصبر على ما یقولون» (The present tense verb signifies continuity).

4- The enemy's mockery and propaganda are very harsh and intense (because they tire a Prophet who possesses broad-mindedness and forbearance, making him in need of patience). «اصبر على...»

5 - Prophets also need Divine admonitions. «اِصبر...»

6- The role of role models in education must not be forgotten. «واذكر عبدنا داود»

7- The source of the power to be patient is servitude and submission to Allah. «عبدنا داود»

8 - Power in the hands of Pharaoh leads to tyranny, «و فرعون ذوالاوتاد» but in the hands of men of God, it is accompanied by humility and servitude. «داود ذاالاید انّه اوّاب»

9- The repentance (tawbah) and turning (inabah) of those in power are worthy of praise. «ذاالاید انّه اوّاب»

10- Repentance and turning are valuable when they are not brief or momentary. «اوّاب» Prophet David frequently turned (inabah) and turned to Allah in all matters of life, and this constant attention was the source of his strength. «ذا الاید انّه اوّاب»

11- Through spiritual perfection, man can bring nature into harmony with himself. «سخّرنا الجبال معه»

12- One revolutionary act by David (mentioned in verse 251 of Surah Al-Baqarah: «و قتل داود جالوت...») brought about all those Divine favors. «سخرنا الجبال معه... شددنا ملكه و آتیناه الحكمة»

13- The best time for the remembrance of Allah is evening and morning. «بالعشىّ والاشراق»

14- The condition for receiving Allah's favors is servitude. «عبدنا - ذاالاید - آتیناه الحكمة»

15- Let the disbelievers persist in their obstinacy and refuse to bow before Allah, but they should know that existence is glorifying Allah. «یسبّحن»

16- Mountains possess a kind of consciousness «معه یسبّحن» and animals recognize time. «معه یسبّحن بالعشىّ...»

17- The axis of the glorification of animals and inanimate objects in existence is the glorification of the friends of Allah. «معه یسبّحن... كلّ له اوّاب»

18- Birds possess a kind of consciousness regarding Allah and existence. «والطیر محشورة كل له اوّاب»

19- Governance must be based on wisdom and justice. «شددنا ملكه و آتیناه الحكمة و فصل الخطاب»

20- Prophet David is one of the Prophets who possessed both prophethood and sovereignty simultaneously. «شددنا ملكه - و آتیناه الحكمة»

21- Religion is not separate from politics, and some Prophets held governance. «ذاالاید - شددنا ملكه - فصل الخطاب»

22- We must also strengthen the governance of the men of God. The phrase «شددنا ملكه» symbolizes that you, too, should strengthen the dominion and governance of His Divine friends.

23- Community leaders must possess certain conditions: constant connection with Allah «انّه اوّاب», strength «شددنا ملكه», wisdom «و آتیناه الحكمة», and the ability to settle disputes due to strong logic. «فصل الخطاب»

Ayahs

۞ وَهَلْ أَتَىٰكَ نَبَؤُا۟ ٱلْخَصْمِ إِذْ تَسَوَّرُوا۟ ٱلْمِحْرَابَ (۲۱)
Has there not come to you the account of the contenders, when they scaled the wall into the sanctuary? (21)
إِذْ دَخَلُوا۟ عَلَىٰ دَاوُۥدَ فَفَزِعَ مِنْهُمْ ۖ قَالُوا۟ لَا تَخَفْ ۖ خَصْمَانِ بَغَىٰ بَعْضُنَا عَلَىٰ بَعْضٍ فَٱحْكُم بَيْنَنَا بِٱلْحَقِّ وَلَا تُشْطِطْ وَٱهْدِنَآ إِلَىٰ سَوَآءِ ٱلصِّرَٰطِ (۲۲)
When they entered into the presence of David, he was alarmed by them. They said, ‘Do not be afraid. [We are only] two contenders: one of us has bullied the other. So judge justly between us, and do not exceed [the bounds of justice], and show us the right path.’ (22)

Key Points

22) Uyun Akhbar al-Rida, Vol. 1, p. 94, Chapter 14, Hadith 1, quoted from Tafsir Rahnamā.

Messages

1- Before speaking, we should create motivation for listening in the audience. «هل اتاك نبأ»

2- Allah invites the Prophet to study past history. «هل اتاك نبأ الخصم»

3- Exciting, tumultuous, and alarming scenes are grounds for haste and confusion in judgment. «تسوّروا المحراب - ففزع»

4- Judging in the mihrab (sanctuary/place of worship) increases its value and sanctity. (The platform for judgment of Hadrat Ali عليه السلام was in the Kufa Mosque, and the place of judgment for Hadrat Dawud was in the mihrab). «تسوّروا المحراب»

5- Natural fear and anxiety also affect monotheists and do not contradict Tawhid (monotheism). «ففزع»

6- Prophets, by virtue of being human, also feel dread from unexpected events. «ففزع»

7- For judgment, both parties to the dispute must be present in court. «خصمان»

8- Prophets have been the reference and refuge for the people. «دخلوا على داود... خصمان بغى...»

9- Reminding the judge at the moment of judgment is a means of protecting him from error. «فاحكم بیننا بالحقّ ولا تشطط و اهدنا»

10- The parties to the dispute must seek the implementation of justice (Haqq), not the preservation of their own interests. «فاحكم بیننا بالحقّ»

11- Leaders and judges of the divine society must be receptive to advice and attentive to truth. «فاحكم بیننا بالحقّ ولا تشطط»

12- The implementation of justice in society leads people to the straight path and keeps them away from extremism and negligence. «اهدنا الى سواء الصراط»

Ayah

إِنَّ هَـٰذَآ أَخِى لَهُۥ تِسْعٌ وَتِسْعُونَ نَعْجَةً وَلِىَ نَعْجَةٌ وَٰحِدَةٌ فَقَالَ أَكْفِلْنِيهَا وَعَزَّنِى فِى ٱلْخِطَابِ (۲۳)
‘This brother of mine has ninety-nine ewes, while I have only a single ewe, and [yet] he says, ‘Commit it to my care,’ and he browbeats me in speech.’ (23)

Key Points

The phrase «اكفلنیها» means "entrust its guardianship to me," and this is a metaphor for giving or gifting. And «عزّنى» (from ‘izzah) means dominance or overpowering.

Messages

1- Conflict does not negate brotherhood. («خصمان بغى», «ان هذا اخى»)

2- Man is greedy and excessive in his demands, and he is never satisfied with worldly possessions. («اكفلنیها») (This is contrary to the theory of some philosophers who consider freedom in achieving desires and instincts as a means of tranquility, claiming that man becomes calm once he is satisfied.)

3- Those who intend to seize the rights of others also prepare the legal and argumentative groundwork for their actions before the court. («عزّنى فى الخطاب»)

4- It is possible that an oppressed person cannot properly articulate his right and is outmatched in speech by the oppressor. Therefore, the judge must seek to uncover the truth and not be deceived by the eloquence of the accused. («عزّنى فى الخطاب»)

Ayahs

قَالَ لَقَدْ ظَلَمَكَ بِسُؤَالِ نَعْجَتِكَ إِلَىٰ نِعَاجِهِۦ ۖ وَإِنَّ كَثِيرًا مِّنَ ٱلْخُلَطَآءِ لَيَبْغِى بَعْضُهُمْ عَلَىٰ بَعْضٍ إِلَّا ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ وَعَمِلُوا۟ ٱلصَّـٰلِحَـٰتِ وَقَلِيلٌ مَّا هُمْ ۗ وَظَنَّ دَاوُۥدُ أَنَّمَا فَتَنَّـٰهُ فَٱسْتَغْفَرَ رَبَّهُۥ وَخَرَّ رَاكِعًا وَأَنَابَ ۩ (۲٤)
He said, ‘He has certainly wronged you by asking your ewe in addition to his own ewes, and indeed many partners bully one another, except such as have faith and do righteous deeds, and few are they.’ Then David knew that We had tested him, whereat he pleaded with his Lord for forgiveness, and fell down in prostration and repented. (24)
فَغَفَرْنَا لَهُۥ ذَٰلِكَ ۖ وَإِنَّ لَهُۥ عِندَنَا لَزُلْفَىٰ وَحُسْنَ مَـَٔابٍ (۲٥)
So We forgave him that, and indeed he has [a station of] nearness with Us and a good destination. (25)

Key Points

The story of the judgment of Prophet David (عليه السلام) is also mentioned in the current Torah, but it differs from what is stated in the Qur'an, which is inconsistent with the status of the Prophets and is nothing more than falsehood and lies.

In Chapter 12 of the Book of Samuel in the Torah, we read: Someone came to David's advisor and said: There were two men in a city, one rich and the other poor. The rich man had many sheep and cattle, and the poor man had only one small lamb. A stranger came to the rich man. He was stingy and did not spend from his own wealth, but instead slaughtered the poor man's lamb to host the stranger. Now, what is David's ruling?

David became angry and said: The person who did this deserves to be killed; he must give four sheep in place of one sheep. The questioner said: O David! You are that man! David understood that the purpose of the question was to imply: Since you already had wives, why did you conspire to take possession of your neighbor's wife? Thus, David became distressed and repented!!

Messages

1- Judgment should not be hasty or based on hearing the statement of only one party. (Prophet David judged upon hearing the statement of one party in the dispute and said: «لقد ظلمك» [He has certainly wronged you], and for this reason, he sought forgiveness from Allah).

2- Excessive demand (greed) is oppression, even if the person does not succeed in acquiring more. «ظلمك بسؤال نعجتك الى نعاجه»

3- A person can own a large amount of wealth. (Prophet David did not criticize the possession of 99 ewes, but rather criticized the brother's oppressive excessive demand). «لقد ظلمك...»

4- In choosing a colleague or partner, the focus of the work must be faith (Iman) and righteous deeds, otherwise, there is a risk of fraud and transgression against the rights of the partners. «كثیرا من الخلطاء لیبغى... الاّ الّذین آمنوا»

5- Faith and righteous deeds are effective when they are together. «آمنوا و عملوا الصالحات»

6- Partnership is a ground for error and wasting the partner's right. «كثیراً من الخلطاء لیبغى»

7- A healthy economy is achieved under the shadow of faith and righteous deeds. «لیبغى... الا الّذین آمنوا و عملوا الصالحات»

8- Those who observe the rights of others are very few. «قلیل ما هم»

9- Trial (testing) is relevant even for the Divine Prophets. «انّما فتنّاه»

10- The recommendation for faith, righteous deeds, and observing people's rights is the best guidance. (They sought guidance from Prophet David: «اهدنا الى سواء الصراط» [Guide us to the straight path]. He said: «كثیراً من الخلطاء لیبغى... الا الّذین آمنوا...»)

11- Prophets demonstrated the best form of repentance (inabah) and seeking forgiveness (tawbah) even for the simplest slip (tark al-awla). (Prophet David's tark al-awla was judging before hearing the statements of both parties). «فاستغفر ربه و خرّ راكعا و اناب»

12- Immediate repentance has great value. «فاستغفر» (The letter fa [ف] implies immediacy).

13- Divine Lordship (Rububiyyah) is the basis for seeking forgiveness. «فاستغفر ربّه»

14- The rights of the people are so important that haste in judgment, even if committed by an infallible Prophet, must be accompanied by seeking forgiveness (Istighfar). «فاستغفر ربّه»

15- Ruku' (bowing) in prayer existed even before Islam. «خرّ راكعاً»

16- Repentance must be both external and internal/profound. «خرّ راكعاً و اناب»

17- Immediate repentance leads to immediate acceptance. «فاستغفر... فغفرنا له»

18- Repentance, besides compensating for the past, also secures the future. «فغفرنا له ذلك و ان له عندنا لزلفى و حسن مأب»

19- Spiritual status is valuable only when accompanied by a good outcome (husn al-'aqibah) and security for the future. «زلفى و حسن مأب»

20- Sometimes this world and the Hereafter are combined for one person. «شددنا ملكه - سخرنا الجبال معه - عندنا لزلفى و حسن مأب»

Ayah

يَـٰدَاوُۥدُ إِنَّا جَعَلْنَـٰكَ خَلِيفَةً فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ فَٱحْكُم بَيْنَ ٱلنَّاسِ بِٱلْحَقِّ وَلَا تَتَّبِعِ ٱلْهَوَىٰ فَيُضِلَّكَ عَن سَبِيلِ ٱللَّهِ ۚ إِنَّ ٱلَّذِينَ يَضِلُّونَ عَن سَبِيلِ ٱللَّهِ لَهُمْ عَذَابٌ شَدِيدٌۢ بِمَا نَسُوا۟ يَوْمَ ٱلْحِسَابِ (۲٦)
‘O David! Indeed, We have made you a vicegerent on the earth. So judge between people with justice, and do not follow your desires, or they will lead you astray from the way of Allah. Indeed there is a severe punishment for those who stray from the way of Allah, because of their forgetting the Day of Reckoning.’ (26)

Messages

1- Allah has entrusted the management of His servants' affairs to the Prophets. «یا داود انّا جعلناك خلیفة»

2- The management of people's affairs is Allah's right, which He delegates to whomever He wills. «انّا جعلناك»

3- Religion is not separate from politics. «یا داود انّا جعلناك خلیفة»

4- Governing the people is a great blessing, the gratitude for which is the implementation of justice among the people. «جعلناك خلیفة... فاحكم بین الناس بالحقّ»

5- The axis of judgment must be truth. «فاحكم بین الناس بالحقّ»

6- The leaders and judges of society must be far from carnal desires. «خلیفة... فاحكم... لا تتبع الهوى»

7- In management, preserve personnel as much as possible. (Allah does not remove His chosen ones from the social sphere merely because of a tark-e awla [leaving the preferable]). «فاستغفر ربه... فغفرنا... یا داود انّا جعلناك»

8- Judgment is among the functions of governance and must be divine. «شددنا ملكه - انّا جعلناك خلیفة - فاحكم»

9- Religious governance has a very long history. «شددنا ملكه - جعلناك خلیفة - فاحكم»

10- The conduct of Allah's Caliph must be a reflection of the Divine actions. «واللّه یقضى بالحقّ»(23), «فاحكم بین الناس بالحقّ»

11- Whatever stands against the truth is caprice and desire. «فاحكم بین الناس بالحقّ ولا تتبع الهوى»

12- The danger of judgment is distance from the truth and attention to carnal desires. «فاحكم... بالحق ولا تتبع الهوى»

13- All human beings, even the Prophets, are exposed to the trap of desire, but they remain protected through Divine warnings. «لا تتبع الهوى»

14- Following desires is forbidden, whether the inclinations are individual and personal or group and partisan. «لا تتبع الهوى»

15- Following desires is equivalent to deviation. «لا تتبع الهوى فیضلّك»

16- Dangers advance step by step (The first step is following desires, the second step is deviation from the path of Allah, the third step is forgetting the reckoning and the Resurrection, resulting in severe punishment). «لا تتبع الهوى فیضلّك... لهم عذاب شدید بما نسوا یوم الحساب»

17- Belief in the Resurrection is not enough; one must remember the Resurrection. (Regarding some individuals, the Quran states: «لا یؤمن بیوم الحساب»(24) [they do not believe in the Day of Reckoning], but in this Ayah, we read: belief exists, but they forget the Resurrection. «نسوا یوم الحساب»

18- Following desires and forgetting the Day of Resurrection are dangers that threaten the rulers of society. «جعلناك خلیفة... لا تتبع الهوى... نسوا یوم الحساب»(23) Ghafir, 20. (24) Ghafir, 27.

Ayahs

وَمَا خَلَقْنَا ٱلسَّمَآءَ وَٱلْأَرْضَ وَمَا بَيْنَهُمَا بَـٰطِلًا ۚ ذَٰلِكَ ظَنُّ ٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا۟ ۚ فَوَيْلٌ لِّلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا۟ مِنَ ٱلنَّارِ (۲۷)
We did not create the sky and the earth and whatever is between them in vain. That is a conjecture of the faithless. So woe to the faithless for the Fire! (27)
أَمْ نَجْعَلُ ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ وَعَمِلُوا۟ ٱلصَّـٰلِحَـٰتِ كَٱلْمُفْسِدِينَ فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ أَمْ نَجْعَلُ ٱلْمُتَّقِينَ كَٱلْفُجَّارِ (۲۸)
Shall We treat those who have faith and do righteous deeds like those who cause corruption on the earth? Shall We treat the Godwary like the vicious? (28)

Messages

1- In the Divine worldview, creation is purposeful. «ما خلقنا... باطلا» And in the non-Divine perspective, creation is without purpose. «ذلك ظن الّذین كفروا»

2- Since the system of existence is based on truth (Haqq), judgment must also be based on truth so that the legal system is consistent with the system of creation. «فاحكم... بالحقّ... و ما خلقنا... باطلا»

3- In all of existence, not a single particle has been created in vain. «ما خلقنا السماء و الارض و ما بینهما باطلا»

4- The disbelievers have no proof for their beliefs; rather, their reliance is on conjecture and assumption. «ظن الّذین...»

5- Since creation is purposeful, it is never abandoned, and it continues until the Day of Judgment and the believer and the corrupt receive their reward and punishment. «و ما خلقنا... باطلا... فویل للّذین كفروا من النار»

6- The reason for the Resurrection (Ma'ad) is Divine wisdom and justice. Wisdom: Nothing has been created in vain. But justice: Shall We treat the wicked and the pious equally? «ما خلقنا... باطلا - ام نجعل المتّقین كالفجّار»

7- Piety (Taqwa) is not withdrawal and seclusion; rather, it is entering the field and working, specifically performing righteous deeds. «الّذین آمنوا و عملوا الصالحات... المتّقین»

8- Corruption (Fasad) is not only murder and crime; rather, sin is a type of corruption. «كالمفسدین... كافجار»

9- If someone views the righteous and the corrupt equally, it is as if he has considered existence vain. «ام نجعل الّذین آمنوا... كالمفسدین»

Ayah

كِتَـٰبٌ أَنزَلْنَـٰهُ إِلَيْكَ مُبَـٰرَكٌ لِّيَدَّبَّرُوٓا۟ ءَايَـٰتِهِۦ وَلِيَتَذَكَّرَ أُو۟لُوا۟ ٱلْأَلْبَـٰبِ (۲۹)
[This is] a blessed Book that We have sent down to you, so that they may contemplate its signs, and that those who possess intellect may take admonition. (29)

Key Points

Mubārak (blessed) refers to something whose benefit and goodness have great and constant growth and increase. The Quran originates from a blessed source. "تبارك الّذى" (Blessed is He Who...). It was revealed on a blessed night. "فى لیلة مباركه" (in a blessed night). It was revealed in a blessed place "ببكة مباركا" (in Mecca, blessed), and the Book itself is blessed. "كتاب... مبارك" (a Book... blessed).

The characteristics of the Quran are outlined in this Ayah: a) Its text is written. "كتاب" (a Book). b) It is from the source of revelation and infinite Divine knowledge. "انزلناه" (We have sent it down). c) Its recipient is an infallible person. "الیك" (to you). d) Its content is full of blessings. "مبارك" (blessed). e) The purpose of its revelation is contemplation upon it. "لیدّبروا" (that they may contemplate). f) Knowledge and awareness of its points and teachings are the prelude to spiritual movement and proximity to Allah. "لیتذكّر" (that they may take heed). g) Those who will achieve this success are the wise. "اولواالالباب" (those of understanding).

The importance of the Quran and contemplation upon it * Whoever does not contemplate the verses of the Quran deserves Divine scorn. "أفلا یتدبّرون القرآن ام على قلوب اقفالها" (Do they not then contemplate the Quran, or are there locks upon their hearts?) (25) * A Divine scholar (‘ālim rabbānī) is one whose work involves studying and teaching the Quran. "... كونوا ربانیین بما كنتم تعلمون الكتاب و بما كنتم تدرسون" (... be scholars of the Lord, because of what you have taught of the Scripture and because of what you have studied.) (26) * The Heavenly Book must be taken seriously. "خذ الكتاب بقوّة" (Take the Scripture with determination.) (27) * Those who abandoned the Quran will be the subject of complaint by the Noble Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله on the Day of Judgment. "و قال الرسول یا ربّ ان قومى اتخذوا هذا القرآن مهجورا" (And the Messenger will say, "O my Lord, indeed my people have taken this Quran as [a thing] abandoned.") (28) * Imam Sajjad عليه السلام said: If I have the Quran and I am alone, I do not feel alienation. "لو مات من بین المشرق و المغرب لما استوحشت بعد ان یكون القرآن معى" (Even if everyone between the East and the West were to die, I would not feel lonely as long as the Quran is with me.) (29) * Hadrat Ali عليه السلام said: The Quran is an ocean whose depth no one can reach. "بحراً لا یدرك قعره" (an ocean whose depth is not reached.) (30) * Imam Khomeini قدس سره deeply regretted why he had not spent his entire life contemplating the Quran (31), just as the late Mulla Sadra expressed this regret in his commentary on Surah Al-Waqi'ah.

25) Muhammad, 24. 26) Aal-i-Imran, 79. 27) Maryam, 12. 28) Al-Furqan, 30. 29) Kafi, Vol. 2, p. 602. 30) Nahj al-Balaghah, Sermon 198. 31) Sahifeh Noor, Vol. 20, p. 20.

Messages

1- The Quran is blessed. (Its recitation, contemplation, history, argumentation, stories, models, teachings, unseen news, metaphors, commands, and prohibitions—all are full of secrets and mysteries.) "كتاب... مبارك" (a Book... blessed).

2- Although the Quran is blessed, it is meant for contemplation, not merely seeking blessings from its physical appearance. (Such as using it to protect the house, the traveler, or the bride from dangers.) "كتاب... مبارك لیدبّروا" (a Book... blessed, that they may contemplate).

3- Contemplation upon the Quran is the prelude to taking heed (tadhakkur); otherwise, a person might understand the scientific secrets and subtleties of the Quran but become arrogant because of them. "یدّبّروا - یتذكّر" (contemplate - take heed).

4- Contemplation must be upon all the verses of the Quran, not just the verses of rulings (Ayat al-Ahkam). "لیدّبروا آیاته" (that they may contemplate its verses).

5- The condition for contemplation, benefit, and acceptance of advice is intellect and wisdom. "لیدّبروا... و لیتذكّر اولواالالباب" (that they may contemplate... and that those of understanding may take heed).

6- Those who do not take heed from the Quran are unwise. "لیتذكّر اولواالالباب" (that those of understanding may take heed).

7- The Quran conforms to intellect and wisdom; therefore, the wise, by contemplating it, realize its rulings and secrets. "لیتذكّر اولوا الالباب" (that those of understanding may take heed). (No matter contrary to reason is found in the Quran.)

8- The teachings of the Quran are endless. The fact that everyone is commanded to contemplate indicates that whoever contemplates will reach a new point. If past scholars and scientists had understood all the secrets of the Quran, our contemplation would be futile. "لیدبّروا آیاته" (that they may contemplate its verses).

Ayahs

وَوَهَبْنَا لِدَاوُۥدَ سُلَيْمَـٰنَ ۚ نِعْمَ ٱلْعَبْدُ ۖ إِنَّهُۥٓ أَوَّابٌ (۳٠)
And to David We gave Solomon—what an excellent servant he was! Indeed, he was a penitent [soul]. (30)
إِذْ عُرِضَ عَلَيْهِ بِٱلْعَشِىِّ ٱلصَّـٰفِنَـٰتُ ٱلْجِيَادُ (۳۱)
One evening when there were displayed before him prancing steeds, (31)
فَقَالَ إِنِّىٓ أَحْبَبْتُ حُبَّ ٱلْخَيْرِ عَن ذِكْرِ رَبِّى حَتَّىٰ تَوَارَتْ بِٱلْحِجَابِ (۳۲)
he said, ‘Indeed I have preferred the love of [worldly] niceties to the remembrance of my Lord until [the sun] disappeared behind the [night’s] veil.’ (32)
رُدُّوهَا عَلَىَّ ۖ فَطَفِقَ مَسْحًۢا بِٱلسُّوقِ وَٱلْأَعْنَاقِ (۳۳)
‘Bring it back for me!’ Then he [and others] began to wipe [their] legs and necks. (33)

Key Points

«صافنات» (Sāfināt) is the plural of «صافنة» (Sāfinah), referring to a horse that, when standing, slightly lifts one of its forelegs and rests only the tip of the hoof on the ground. This is a sign of its agility. «جیاد» (Jiyād), the plural of «جواد» (Jawād), means a swift horse, or the plural of «جَیّد» (Jayyid), meaning precious and valuable. The word «خیر» (Khayr) is also used regarding worldly possessions, and here it refers to the horses. The phrase «حُبّ الخیر» (Hubb al-Khayr) means loving something that brings goodness to a person, just as He says elsewhere: «و انه لحب الخیر لشدید» (32)

An Explanation and Review Regarding These Verses

Two completely different interpretations have been presented regarding these verses. According to what we have stated:

One day, in the afternoon, Prophet Sulayman (عليه السلام) was reviewing the swift horses he had prepared for Jihad, similar to the review we read about in Ayah 17 of Surah An-Naml: «و حشر لسلیمان جنوده من الجنّ و الانس» (And gathered for Sulayman were his hosts of jinn and men). Sulayman's affection for the horses was for military purposes and for the sake of Allah. Therefore, he said: The source of my affection is the remembrance of Allah. «احببت حب الخیر عن ذكر ربّى» (I loved the love of goodness for the remembrance of my Lord). Sulayman continued watching until the horses disappeared from his sight. He ordered the horses to be brought back again and the parade to be repeated. During the second review, Sulayman stroked the necks and shanks of the horses, thereby also showing appreciation to the trainers.

This interpretation is consistent with the apparent meaning of the verse, and both Fakhr Razi and Sayyid Murtada have narrated it. It is also inferred from the statement of the late Allamah Majlisi in Bihar (Vol. 14, p. 104). Based on this interpretation, the phrase «توارت بالحجاب» (they disappeared behind the veil) refers to the horses moving away from the review area and the sight of Prophet Sulayman, and the phrase «ردّوها علىّ» (Bring them back to me) refers to the request to repeat the review.

However, in some interpretations, we read: Sulayman became so engrossed in watching the horses that the sun set and he missed his afternoon prayer ('Asr). Therefore, he became distressed and ordered the sun to return, and he performed ablution (Wudu), wiping his head, neck, and feet.

There are objections to this meaning, including:

  1. There is no mention of the sun in the verse for «توارت بالحجاب» to mean its setting, or for «ردّوها علىّ» to mean its return.
  2. How can a Prophet whom Allah praises in the previous verse with the phrases «نعم العبد» (the excellent servant) and «اوّاب» (oft-returning) be introduced in the next verse as someone negligent of prayer!?
  3. What justification is there for the commanding tone used to order the sun's return, «ردّوها علىّ» (Bring them back to me)? If the missed prayer was obligatory, it is inconsistent with the Prophet's status. If it was a supererogatory (Nāfilah) prayer, what justification is there for the sun to return for a supererogatory prayer?

This interpretation is also mentioned in some narrations, but the mentioned narrations do not have a sound chain of transmission (sanad) and are inconsistent with rational principles which state that Prophets must be infallible (ma'sum). Therefore, it is better to leave the understanding of these narrations to those qualified to interpret them. (33)

32) Adiyat, 8. 33) Tafsir-e Nemooneh.

Messages

1- A child is a Divine gift. «وهبنا» 2- The focus of Allah's praise for man is his servitude. «نعم العبد انّه اوّاب» 3- Bitter and sweet circumstances do not affect the friends of Allah. (The phrase «نعم العبد» is used both for Prophet Dawud and Sulayman, who had all facilities available to them, and for Prophet Ayyub, who faced all bitter events.) 4- Attention and return to Allah must be continuous. «اوّاب» 5- Reviewing military capabilities and forces is a commendable action. «عرض علیه...» 6- The leader must personally review the forces. Awareness of the quantity and quality of forces and capabilities is a prerequisite for leadership. «عرض علیه» 7- The highest authority inspects animals, which carries both a managerial message and a message of humility. «عرض علیه بالصافنات الجیاد» 8- Parades and reviews are necessary for combat forces. «عرض علیه...» As He said elsewhere: Sulayman's armies paraded before him. «جنوده من الجنّ والانس» (34) 9- Affection and attachment to wealth and resources are permissible if they are purposeful and balanced. «احببت حبّ الخیر عن ذكر ربّى» Prophet Sulayman says in this verse: My affection for the horses stems from the love of God. (Because the better equipped the army of Monotheism is, the greater the honor.) 10- Warhorses were beloved by the Divine Prophets. «احببت حبّ الخیر» Just as the Quran swears by the breath of the horses. «و العادیات ضبحاً» 11- Sometimes it is necessary to repeat the inspection. «ردّوها علىّ» 12- Animals sense the wiping and stroking of humans. «فطفق مسحاً» 13- Compassion for animals and petting them is the work of the Prophets. «فطفق مسحا بالسّوق والاعناق» 14- The review must be accompanied by gentleness. «عرض علیه - فطفق مسحاً»

34) Naml, 17.

Ayahs

وَلَقَدْ فَتَنَّا سُلَيْمَـٰنَ وَأَلْقَيْنَا عَلَىٰ كُرْسِيِّهِۦ جَسَدًا ثُمَّ أَنَابَ (۳٤)
Certainly We tried Solomon, and cast a [lifeless] body on his throne. Thereupon he was penitent. (34)
قَالَ رَبِّ ٱغْفِرْ لِى وَهَبْ لِى مُلْكًا لَّا يَنۢبَغِى لِأَحَدٍ مِّنۢ بَعْدِىٓ ۖ إِنَّكَ أَنتَ ٱلْوَهَّابُ (۳٥)
He said, ‘My Lord! Forgive me, and grant me a kingdom that will not befit anyone except me. Indeed You are the All-munificent.’ (35)

Key Points

These verses speak about the severe trial (test) of Prophet Solomon (عليه السلام). However, there are several views regarding the nature of this trial:

A) Solomon had several wives and wished to be granted many strong children who would be His support in administering the kingdom. However, He became heedless of Allah and did not say "Insha'Allah" (ان شاء اللّه). Consequently, no child was born to Him except for a physically incomplete child (ناقص الخلقه) which was cast upon His throne like a lifeless body (جسدى بى روح). B) Allah afflicted Solomon Himself with a severe illness, causing Him to lie upon the throne like a lifeless body (جسدى بى روح). Then He restored Him to His original state and cured Him. (Considering that anāb (اناب) means 'to return'). C) Allah cast the corpse of His son, whom He dearly loved, upon His throne.

Messages

1- All people, even the Prophets, are tested. «فتنّا سلیمان»

2- Trials are a means of polishing the soul and achieving proximity to Allah. «فتنّا... ثمّ اناب»

3- The monopolization of power by infallible and divine individuals does not lead to tyranny or deviation. «هب لى ملكاً لا ینبغى لاحد»

4- First, we must purify ourselves through repentance and turning back (to Allah), and cleanse the vessel of the soul; then we should ask Allah for governance and power. «ربّ اغفرلى و هب لى»

5- Seeking forgiveness (Istighfār) before supplication (Du'ā) is a cause for the acceptance of the supplication. «ربّ اغفرلى و هب لى ملكاً»

6- Some Prophets sought power and governance. «هب لى ملكاً»

7- The power of the Prophets is not achieved through force or coup, but through Divine favors. «هب لى ملكاً»

8- Acquiring spirituality, turning back (to Allah), and humility does not necessitate isolation and seclusion. «ثمّ اناب... هب لى ملكاً»

9- Non-involvement in politics creates sanctity. «هب لى ملكاً»

10- Do not ask Allah only for the removal of problems and the healing of illness; rather, in addition to compensating and reforming the past, also request the acquisition of power and the salvation of the Ummah. «ثمّ اناب... هب لى ملكاً»

11- The high ambition of Prophet Solomon caused Him to make lofty requests from Allah. «ملكاً لا ینبغى لاحد»

12- In supplication (Du'ā) and intimate prayer (Munājāt), mention the Attributes and Names of Allah that are appropriate to the request. «هب لى - انت الوهّاب»

13- Extensive and continuous granting (of gifts) belongs exclusively to Allah. «انّك انت الوهاب»

Ayahs

فَسَخَّرْنَا لَهُ ٱلرِّيحَ تَجْرِى بِأَمْرِهِۦ رُخَآءً حَيْثُ أَصَابَ (۳٦)
So We disposed the wind for him, blowing softly wherever he intended by his command, (36)
وَٱلشَّيَـٰطِينَ كُلَّ بَنَّآءٍ وَغَوَّاصٍ (۳۷)
and every builder and diver from the demons, (37)
وَءَاخَرِينَ مُقَرَّنِينَ فِى ٱلْأَصْفَادِ (۳۸)
and others [too] bound together in chains. (38)
هَـٰذَا عَطَآؤُنَا فَٱمْنُنْ أَوْ أَمْسِكْ بِغَيْرِ حِسَابٍ (۳۹)
‘This is Our bounty: so withhold or bestow without any reckoning.’ (39)
وَإِنَّ لَهُۥ عِندَنَا لَزُلْفَىٰ وَحُسْنَ مَـَٔابٍ (٤٠)
Indeed he has [a station of] nearness with Us and a good destination. (40)

Key Points

The word rukhā’ (رُخاء) means ease. This wind would lift Solomon's throne and gently set it down wherever he willed. The intent of al-Shayāṭīn (الشیاطین) is either the Jinn devils who were subjugated to Solomon, despite their nature being rebellion and defiance, or it is broader, including rebellious humans and defiant Jinn, because the word Shayṭān (devil) is also used in the Qur'an with this broad meaning: "Shayāṭīn al-Ins wa al-Jinn" (devils among mankind and jinn) (35). By His miracle, Allah placed all natural, human, and Jinn forces under Solomon's authority. One group built what he desired on land (bannā’ - builder), and another group fulfilled the needs of his government in the sea (ghawwāṣ - diver). The intent of "hādha ‘aṭā’unā... bi-ghayri ḥisāb" (هذا عطاؤنا... بغیر حساب) is the boundless Divine gift to the prophets, which is not subject to reckoning or accountability. In these verses, Allah refers to the favors He bestowed upon Prophet Solomon, including: the subjugation of the wind, the utilization of Jinn power, the restraint of destructive forces, the delegation of authority, the station of Divine proximity, and a brilliant future. These blessings are part of the exemplary government that he had requested. Indeed, an exemplary government must be safe from destructive forces (muqarraneen fī al-aṣfād - bound in chains); it must solve the problem of rapid communication and transport (sakhkharnā lahu al-rīḥ bi-amrih - We subjected the wind to him by his command); it must utilize expertise (bannā’ wa ghawwāṣ - builder and diver); it must have the power to decide (fa-amnun aw amsik - so bestow favor or withhold); its hand must be open (bi-ghayri ḥisāb - without reckoning); and at the same time, its spiritual future must be secured (‘indanā la-zulfā wa ḥusnu ma’āb - surely for him is nearness to Us and a good return). Governance is a slippery slope leading to deviation and a bad end. Therefore, Prophet Joseph, while in the well and the prison, did not say: tawaffanī musliman (cause me to die a Muslim). However, as soon as he attained governance, he requested a good end and to die as a Muslim from Allah: "Rabbi qad ātaytanī min al-mulki wa ‘allamtanī min ta’wīl al-aḥādīthi fāṭir al-samāwāti wa al-arḍi anta waliyyī fī al-dunyā wa al-ākhirati tawaffanī musliman wa alḥiqnī bi-al-ṣāliḥīn" (My Lord, You have given me [a share] of the sovereignty and taught me the interpretation of narratives. Creator of the heavens and the earth, You are my Protector in this world and the Hereafter. Cause me to die a Muslim and join me with the righteous) (36). And Prophet Solomon also had a good end (ḥusnu ma’āb - a good return).

35) An'am, 112. 36) Yusuf, 101.

Messages

1- Allah answers the prayers of the righteous. Hab lī mulkan... fa-sakhkharnā lahu al-rīḥ (Grant me sovereignty... so We subjected the wind to him).

2- The Lord's response is a sign of the permissibility of Solomon's request for unparalleled sovereignty. Hab lī mulkan... fa-sakhkharnā lahu al-rīḥ.

3- By Allah's Will, nature can be both beneficial and harmful. Bi-rīḥin ṣarṣar (with a fierce wind) (37), sakhkharnā lahu al-rīḥ (We subjected the wind to him).

4- Prophets possess cosmic authority (wilāyah takwīniyyah). Tajrī bi-amrih... ḥaythu aṣāb (running by his command... wherever he directed).

5- Compared to the government of Prophet Solomon, the world's industry is still very backward. Sakhkharnā lahu al-rīḥ (We subjected the wind to him) - wa al-shayāṭīn kullu bannā’in wa ghawwāṣ (and the devils, every builder and diver).

6- The Jinn can work for the benefit of mankind. Al-shayāṭīn kullu bannā’in wa ghawwāṣ.

7- Even rebellious forces can be utilized through proper management. Al-shayāṭīn kullu bannā’in wa ghawwāṣ.

8- The Jinn possess intellect, consciousness, and expertise. Bannā’ wa ghawwāṣ.

9- In addition to the power to dominate other humans and nature, mankind also has the power to dominate Jinn and devils. Bannā’ wa ghawwāṣ.

10- The sea is a source of wealth and power. Wa al-shayāṭīn kullu bannā’in wa ghawwāṣ.

11- In management, all forces must be mobilized to execute important projects. Kullu bannā’in wa ghawwāṣ.

12- There is no objection to utilizing the expertise of foreigners and non-believers. Al-shayāṭīn kullu bannā’in wa ghawwāṣ.

13- Imprisonment is necessary even in a Divine government. Since some Jinn were builders and divers while others were bound in chains, it is clear that this latter group would have caused disruption in the government of Solomon عليه السلام if they had been free. Muqarraneen fī al-aṣfād.

14- In Divine governments, power and authority are delegated by Allah to the ruler. Sakhkharnā (We subjected) - hādha ‘aṭā’unā fa-amnun aw amsik (This is Our gift, so bestow favor or withhold).

15- Between giving and withholding, precedence is given to generosity. (Fa-amnun (bestow favor) comes before amsik (withhold)).

16- A government is Divine when security, industry, management, and natural resources are combined with spiritual status. Sakhkharnā (We subjected) - bannā’ wa ghawwāṣ (builder and diver) - wa inna lahu ‘indanā la-zulfā (and surely for him is nearness to Us).

17- Material development (bannā’ wa ghawwāṣ) does not contradict the station of Divine proximity. Thumma anāb (then he turned [to Us]) - qāla rabbi ighfir (he said, "My Lord, forgive") - inna lahu ‘indanā la-zulfā.

18- Most governments show a green light and enjoy relative popularity at the beginning of their work, but their popularity and power gradually diminish. However, Solomon's government had a good end. Ḥusnu ma’āb.

37) Haqqah, 6.

Ayahs

وَٱذْكُرْ عَبْدَنَآ أَيُّوبَ إِذْ نَادَىٰ رَبَّهُۥٓ أَنِّى مَسَّنِىَ ٱلشَّيْطَـٰنُ بِنُصْبٍ وَعَذَابٍ (٤۱)
And remember Our servant Job [in the Quran]. When he called out to his Lord, ‘The devil has visited on me hardship and torment,’ (41)
ٱرْكُضْ بِرِجْلِكَ ۖ هَـٰذَا مُغْتَسَلٌۢ بَارِدٌ وَشَرَابٌ (٤۲)
[We told him:] ‘Stamp your foot on the ground; this [ensuing spring] will be a cooling bath and drink.’ (42)

Key Points

Nusub (نُصب) means hardship and difficulty. Rakd (رَكض) is striking the foot upon the ground. Mughtasal (مغتسل) refers to water or the place of washing.

As mentioned in narrations: Prophet Ayyub (Job) had great wealth, children, and resources, and he constantly thanked Allah. Satan presented to Allah that if He saw Ayyub as grateful, it was because of the abundant blessings He had given him; if these blessings were taken away from him, he would not remain a thankful servant. Allah permitted Satan to dominate Ayyub's worldly possessions. Satan first afflicted Ayyub's wealth, sheep, and crops with calamity and disaster, but this had no effect on Ayyub. Then Satan gained dominance over Ayyub's body, and he became so ill that he was confined to bed due to the severity of pain and suffering. Yet, despite this, nothing was diminished from his state of gratitude. Since Ayyub successfully passed this severe Divine test, Allah restored His blessings to him and he regained his physical health. (38)

To restore health to Ayyub's body, Allah commanded him to strike his foot upon the ground, thereby causing a spring of water to flow by Divine power, and by washing his body in that water, to cleanse his body of all illness.

The endurance of hardship by the Prophets has blessings, including: a) People do not fall into exaggeration (ghuluw) concerning them. b) They receive a special status as a result of their patience. c) If role models and great figures also experience hardship, the poor, the weak, and the sick are not humiliated. d) People learn from them how to deal with problems.

Based on narrations, some people assumed that Prophet Ayyub had committed a sin that caused him to be afflicted by such a calamity, and thus they gloated over him. Whereas, calamity for the Awliya (saints/close servants of God) is a means of growth and attaining higher status. (39)

In this Surah, the distinct stories of two Divine Prophets are presented: one is Prophet Sulayman (Solomon), a Prophet who possessed the highest power and resources. The other is Prophet Ayyub, a Prophet who tasted the bitterest hardships for a long period. However, the Quran praises both Prophets with the phrase “Ni’mal ‘Abd” (نعم العبد - the best servant). This is because neither prosperity nor suffering could remove them from the path of servitude to Allah. Indeed, a skilled diver and fisherman performs his job excellently whether in a salty sea or a fresh sea, and the salinity or sweetness of the water does not affect his diving or fishing.

38) Tafsir-e Namuneh. 39) Bihar, Vol. 12, p. 351.

Messages

1- The history of great men should not be forgotten. The history of the Prophets is constructive even for the Prophet of Islam, because familiarity with the problems of others strengthens patience and endurance. «واذكر عبدنا ایوب»

2- Ayyub was a true servant of Allah. «عبدنا»

3- Complaining about problems to Allah is permissible. «نادى ربّه انّى مسّنى الشیطان»

4- Satan has no dominance over the spirit and belief of Allah’s Awliya (saints), but he can play an annoying role concerning their physical bodies. «مسّنى الشیطان بنصب»

5- Hardships and calamities are suitable grounds for the whispers of Satans. «بنصب» (Based on the meaning that: Satan contacts me because of the hardships.)

6- Supplication (Du'a) is a manifestation of servitude. «عبدنا ایوب اذ نادى ربّه»

7- We should supplicate to remove afflictions and problems. «نادى ربّه انّى مسّنى الشیطان»

8- The supplications of the Prophets are answered, and relief follows hardships. «اركض برجلك»

9- Experiencing hardships is not always a sign of distance from Allah. (Sometimes Allah’s Awliya endure the most difficult conditions, while a spring of cool water flows for them by striking their foot upon the ground.) «هذا مغتسل بارد»

10- Washing with cold water plays an important role in health. «مغتسل بارد و شراب» (The washing water must also be clean and drinkable).

11- Nature is subjugated to Allah, and Allah subjugates it to His Awliya. «اركض برجلك هذا مغتسل بارد و شراب»

Ayahs

وَوَهَبْنَا لَهُۥٓ أَهْلَهُۥ وَمِثْلَهُم مَّعَهُمْ رَحْمَةً مِّنَّا وَذِكْرَىٰ لِأُو۟لِى ٱلْأَلْبَـٰبِ (٤۳)
We gave [back] his family to him along with others like them, as a mercy from Us and an admonition for those who possess intellect. (43)
وَخُذْ بِيَدِكَ ضِغْثًا فَٱضْرِب بِّهِۦ وَلَا تَحْنَثْ ۗ إِنَّا وَجَدْنَـٰهُ صَابِرًا ۚ نِّعْمَ ٱلْعَبْدُ ۖ إِنَّهُۥٓ أَوَّابٌ (٤٤)
[We told him:] ‘Take a faggot in your hand and then strike [your wife] with it, but do not break [your] oath.’ Indeed, We found him to be patient. What an excellent servant! Indeed he was a penitent [soul]. (44)

Key Points

Dhighth (ضغث) refers to a bundle of thin branches or sticks. Hinth (حنث) means breaking a covenant or an oath. As mentioned in narrations: Ayyub's wife was a devoted woman who did not abandon him throughout his illness and nursed him. One day, Satan appeared to her in the form of a man and said: I will cure your husband on the condition that he accepts that I was the cause of his recovery. When she told Ayyub this proposal, he became angry and said: You have been deceived by Satan and have accepted such a thing. He then swore an oath to punish his wife.

Messages

1- Never despair of Allah's mercy during hardships. «وَهَبْنَا لَهُ أَهْلَهُ... رَحْمَةً مِّنَّا»

2- An extended family is a divine blessing. «وَمِثْلَهُم مَّعَهُمْ»

3- Relief and ease are divine grace, not our privilege or entitlement. «رَحْمَةً مِّنَّا»

4- Only the wise take lessons from ups and downs. «ذِكْرَى لِأُولِي الْأَلْبَابِ»

5- We must learn the way to mitigate punishments from Allah. «خُذْ بِيَدِكَ ضِغْثًا»

6- Even a good record, devotion, and being from the family of prophethood do not prevent the application of the law. «فَاضْرِب بِهِ وَلَا تَحْنَثْ»

7- In difficult circumstances, seek to solve the problem, but do not break the law. «فَاضْرِب بِهِ وَلَا تَحْنَثْ»

8- We must preserve the sanctity of covenants and oaths. «وَلَا تَحْنَثْ»

9- Patience is bitter, but its outcome yields sweet fruit. «وَهَبْنَا لَهُ - وَجَدْنَاهُ صَابِرًا»

10- Repentance (Inabah) and supplication to Allah are the source of patience. «صَابِرًا - إِنَّهُ أَوَّابٌ»

Ayahs

وَٱذْكُرْ عِبَـٰدَنَآ إِبْرَٰهِيمَ وَإِسْحَـٰقَ وَيَعْقُوبَ أُو۟لِى ٱلْأَيْدِى وَٱلْأَبْصَـٰرِ (٤٥)
And remember Our servants, Abraham, Isaac and Jacob, men of strength and insight. (45)
إِنَّآ أَخْلَصْنَـٰهُم بِخَالِصَةٍ ذِكْرَى ٱلدَّارِ (٤٦)
Indeed We purified them with exclusive remembrance of the abode [of the Hereafter]. (46)
وَإِنَّهُمْ عِندَنَا لَمِنَ ٱلْمُصْطَفَيْنَ ٱلْأَخْيَارِ (٤۷)
Indeed they are surely with Us among the elect of the best. (47)
وَٱذْكُرْ إِسْمَـٰعِيلَ وَٱلْيَسَعَ وَذَا ٱلْكِفْلِ ۖ وَكُلٌّ مِّنَ ٱلْأَخْيَارِ (٤۸)
And remember Ishmael, Elisha and Dhu’l-Kifl—each [of whom was] among the elect. (48)

Key Points

In these verses, the names of six prophets (Abraham, Isaac, Jacob, Ishmael, Al-Yasa', and Dhul-Kifl) are mentioned, and six attributes are listed for them: servitude to Allah, power, insight, sincerity, chosenness, and goodness. Muṣṭafīn (مصطفین) is the plural of Muṣṭafā (مصطفى), meaning "the chosen one." Akhyār (اخیار) is the plural of Khayr (خیّر), meaning "people of goodness and virtue." Al-Yasa' (الیسع), whose name appears twice in the Quran, is one of the Divine Prophets. In a narration from Imam Riḍā عليه السلام, it is stated that this Prophet, like Prophet Jesus, used to bring the dead back to life. Imam Jawād عليه السلام, in response to Abdul Azīm al-Ḥasanī, said: Dhul-Kifl, after Solomon, was among the 313 prominent Messengers who judged like David and never became angry except for the sake of Allah. (40)

40) Biḥār, Vol. 12, p. 348.

Messages

1- We must keep alive the history of the pure ones, the Prophets, and the great figures. «واذكر عبادنا»

2- The source of all the Prophets' perfections is servitude (to Allah). «عبادنا» is mentioned before their other perfections.

3- A leader must possess power and insight. «اولى الایدى والابصار»

4- A sign of Allah's special favor upon His servant is that the servant constantly remembers the Day of Judgment. «اخلصناهم... ذكرى الدّار»

5- Thinking of the Hereafter and avoiding worldliness are characteristics of Divine leaders. «انا اخلصناهم بخالصة ذكرى الدّار»

6- Remembering the Day of Judgment is a means for man to approach sincerity (Ikhlāṣ). «اخلصناهم... ذكرى الدّار»

7- Thinking of the Hereafter grants man insight. «اولى الا یدى والابصار... انا اخلصناهم...»

8- The highest value is having honor in the sight of Allah. «عندنا لمن المصطفین»

9- If someone is a sincere and good servant of Allah, Allah honors his name. (You should not worry about having a good name.) «واذكر - واذكر...»

10- One of the ways to invite others to goodness is by honoring the people of goodness. «واذكر...»

Ayahs

هَـٰذَا ذِكْرٌ ۚ وَإِنَّ لِلْمُتَّقِينَ لَحُسْنَ مَـَٔابٍ (٤۹)
This is a Reminder, and indeed the Godwary have a good destination: (49)
جَنَّـٰتِ عَدْنٍ مُّفَتَّحَةً لَّهُمُ ٱلْأَبْوَٰبُ (٥٠)
the Gardens of Eden, whose gates will be flung open for them. (50)
مُتَّكِـِٔينَ فِيهَا يَدْعُونَ فِيهَا بِفَـٰكِهَةٍ كَثِيرَةٍ وَشَرَابٍ (٥۱)
Reclining therein [on couches], they will call for abundant fruits and drinks, (51)
۞ وَعِندَهُمْ قَـٰصِرَٰتُ ٱلطَّرْفِ أَتْرَابٌ (٥۲)
and there will be with them maidens of restrained glances, of a like age. (52)
هَـٰذَا مَا تُوعَدُونَ لِيَوْمِ ٱلْحِسَابِ (٥۳)
This is what you are promised on the Day of Reckoning. (53)
إِنَّ هَـٰذَا لَرِزْقُنَا مَا لَهُۥ مِن نَّفَادٍ (٥٤)
This is Our provision, which will never be exhausted. (54)

Messages

1- The narration of history should not be mere entertainment; rather, it must be purposeful and a means of vigilance. «واذكر... هذا ذكر»

2- Dhikr (remembrance) has various dimensions: the Quran is Dhikr, «نحن نزّلنا الذكر»(41) prayer is Dhikr, «اقم الصلاة لذكرى»(42) and the history of great figures is also Dhikr. «هذا ذكر» (The history of great figures is a means of remembrance and learning lessons).

3- Piety (Taqwa) is the means to a favorable outcome. «للمتّقین لحسن مآب» (The word ma'āb means destination and return).

4- To attract people, do not suffice with general statements. (It did not suffice only with «حسن مآب», but was followed by: «جنّات عدن...»)

5- Knowing past history and paying attention to future attractions are effective in guiding people toward the Truth. «واذكر... حسن مأب جنّات عدن»

6- All types of blessings are gathered in Paradise: multiplicity of gardens «جنّات», eternity «عدن», ease of access «مفتحة لهم الابواب», and abundance and plenty «فاكهة كثیرة».

7- The sign of a good spouse is that she does not fix her gaze upon anyone other than her husband. «قاصرات الطرف»

8- The blessings of Paradise are both subject to accountability «یوم الحساب» and are permanent. «ماله من نفاد»

9- The Resurrection (Ma'ād) is physical. (Leaning upon couches, eating fruit, drinking wine, and having spouses—all of these are signs that the Resurrection is physical). «متكئین، فاكهة، شراب، قاصرات الطرف»(41) حجر، ۹.

(42) طه، ۱۴.

Ayahs

هَـٰذَا ۚ وَإِنَّ لِلطَّـٰغِينَ لَشَرَّ مَـَٔابٍ (٥٥)
This [will be for the righteous], and as for the rebellious there will surely be a bad destination: (55)
جَهَنَّمَ يَصْلَوْنَهَا فَبِئْسَ ٱلْمِهَادُ (٥٦)
hell, which they shall enter, an evil resting place. (56)
هَـٰذَا فَلْيَذُوقُوهُ حَمِيمٌ وَغَسَّاقٌ (٥۷)
[They will be told, ‘This is scalding water and pus; let them taste it, (57)
وَءَاخَرُ مِن شَكْلِهِۦٓ أَزْوَٰجٌ (٥۸)
and other kinds [of torments] resembling it.’ (58)
هَـٰذَا فَوْجٌ مُّقْتَحِمٌ مَّعَكُمْ ۖ لَا مَرْحَبًۢا بِهِمْ ۚ إِنَّهُمْ صَالُوا۟ ٱلنَّارِ (٥۹)
[The leaders of the faithless will be told,] ‘This is a group [of your followers] plunging [into hell] along with you.’ [They will repond,] ‘May wretchedness be their lot! For they will enter the Fire.’ (59)
قَالُوا۟ بَلْ أَنتُمْ لَا مَرْحَبًۢا بِكُمْ ۖ أَنتُمْ قَدَّمْتُمُوهُ لَنَا ۖ فَبِئْسَ ٱلْقَرَارُ (٦٠)
They will say, ‘No, may wretchedness be your lot! You prepared this [hell] for us. What an evil abode!’ (60)
قَالُوا۟ رَبَّنَا مَن قَدَّمَ لَنَا هَـٰذَا فَزِدْهُ عَذَابًا ضِعْفًا فِى ٱلنَّارِ (٦۱)
They will say, ‘Our Lord! Whoever has prepared this for us, double his punishment in the Fire!’ (61)

Key Points

“Mihād” (مِهاد) derived from “Mahd” (مَهد) means a place or resting place. “Hamīm” (حمیم) means boiling water, and “Ghassāq” (غسّاق) means the pus and fluid that drips from the skin of the inhabitants of Hell. “Muqtaḥam” (مقتحم) refers to entering into a terrifying matter. “Shakl” (شَكل) means likeness or similarity, and “Azwāj” (ازواج) means types. Meaning, they have other types of punishment of the same kind.

Messages

1- The headline of goodness is piety (taqwa), and the headline of evil is transgression and rebellion (ṭughyān). «ان للمتّقین لحسن مأب - انّ للطاغین لشرّ مأب»

2- Transgression is the cause of a bad outcome. «للطاغین لشرمأب»

3- The punishments of Hell are varied and diverse. «و اخر من شكله ازواج»

4- On the Day of Judgment, the followers of corruption will despise their predecessors. «انتم قدّمتموه لنا»

5- Man sends forth his own Hell in advance. «قدّمتموه لنا»

6- Inviting others to sin does not absolve the sinner of responsibility. Although they say: «انتم قدّمتموه لنا» (You brought this forth for us), they themselves are also trapped in Hell.

7- In the Islamic educational system, encouragement and threat exist side by side. (The verses regarding the reward of the pious and the punishment of the transgressors.)

8- Those who do not pray for one another in this world, within the circle of faith, will curse one another in the Hereafter and in Hell. «ربّنا من قدّم لنا»

9- The only prayer from the inhabitants of Hell that is answered is the prayer to increase the punishment for the pioneers of disbelief. «زده عذاباً ضعفا» «لكل ضعف»(43)

10- Exchanging insults is one of the interactions of the inhabitants of Hell. (One group says: «لا مرحبا بكم» (No welcome for you), but they hear in response: «بل انتم لا مرحبا بكم» (Rather, no welcome for you).)43) A'raf, 39.

Ayahs

وَقَالُوا۟ مَا لَنَا لَا نَرَىٰ رِجَالًا كُنَّا نَعُدُّهُم مِّنَ ٱلْأَشْرَارِ (٦۲)
And they will say, ‘Why is it that we do not see [here] men whom we used to count among the bad ones, (62)
أَتَّخَذْنَـٰهُمْ سِخْرِيًّا أَمْ زَاغَتْ عَنْهُمُ ٱلْأَبْصَـٰرُ (٦۳)
ridiculing them, or do [our] eyes miss them [here]?’ (63)
إِنَّ ذَٰلِكَ لَحَقٌّ تَخَاصُمُ أَهْلِ ٱلنَّارِ (٦٤)
That is indeed a true account of the contentions of the inmates of the Fire. (64)

Key Points

It is narrated in reliable traditions that: A group of Shi'a complained to the Ahl al-Bayt (عليه السلام) that some people call them Rafidhi (rejecters) and consider them worse than polytheists and disbelievers. The Imam (عليه السلام) swore that they (the Shi'a) would be included in their intercession, and those (who insulted them) would look for them in Hellfire, then recited this Ayah: «و قالوا مالنا لا نرى رجالا كنّا نعدّهم من الاشرار».(44)

A portrait of the dispute among the criminals on the Day of Judgment, where everyone seeks a partner in crime to shift the burden of sin onto him: Some will say: Society led us astray. «لولا انتم لكنّا مؤمنین»(45) Some will say: A bad friend led us astray. «لقد اضلّنى عن الذكر»(46) Some will say: The associate led us astray. «الاخلاّء یومئذ بعضهم لبعض عدوّ»(47) Some will say: Corrupt leaders led us astray. «اطعنا سادتنا و كبرائنا فاضلّونا»(48) Some will say: Satan led us astray. «فلا تلومونى و لوموا انفسكم»(49)

44) Tafasir Atayab al-Bayan, Nur al-Thaqalayn, and Kanz al-Daqa'iq. 45) Saba, 31. 46) Furqan, 29. 47) Zukhruf, 67. 48) Al-Ahzab, 67. 49) Ibrahim, 22.

Messages

1- Some people who are considered wicked and are humiliated in this world will be successful in Paradise. «لا نرى رجالاً نعدهم من الاشرار»

2- In Hellfire, people recognize one another and possess awareness and senses. «مالنا لا نرى رجالاً»

3- Let us not judge hastily; perhaps those who appear lowly today will be highly ranked on the Day of Judgment. «نعدّهم من الاشرار»

4- The Day of Judgment is the day of admission. «اتخذناهم سخریّا»

5- The people of Paradise are reclining on couches in a gathering of intimacy, while the people of Hellfire are arguing with one another. «متّكئین فیها», «انّ ذلك لحقّ تخاصم اهل النار»

Ayahs

قُلْ إِنَّمَآ أَنَا۠ مُنذِرٌ ۖ وَمَا مِنْ إِلَـٰهٍ إِلَّا ٱللَّهُ ٱلْوَٰحِدُ ٱلْقَهَّارُ (٦٥)
Say, ‘I am just a warner, and there is no god except Allah, the One, the All-paramount, (65)
رَبُّ ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ وَٱلْأَرْضِ وَمَا بَيْنَهُمَا ٱلْعَزِيزُ ٱلْغَفَّـٰرُ (٦٦)
the Lord of the heavens and the earth and whatever is between them, the All-mighty, the All-forgiver.’ (66)
قُلْ هُوَ نَبَؤٌا۟ عَظِيمٌ (٦۷)
Say, ‘It is a great prophesy, (67)
أَنتُمْ عَنْهُ مُعْرِضُونَ (٦۸)
of which you are disregardful. (68)
مَا كَانَ لِىَ مِنْ عِلْمٍۭ بِٱلْمَلَإِ ٱلْأَعْلَىٰٓ إِذْ يَخْتَصِمُونَ (٦۹)
I have no knowledge of the Supernal Elite when they contend. (69)
إِن يُوحَىٰٓ إِلَىَّ إِلَّآ أَنَّمَآ أَنَا۠ نَذِيرٌ مُّبِينٌ (۷٠)
All that is revealed to me is that I am just a manifest warner.’ (70)

Key Points

"Al-Qahhar" (The Subduer/Dominant) means the overwhelming power before which all other powers are subdued and subject to Him. We read in narrations that the "Great News" (نبأ عظیم) is Ali ibn Abi Talib, and the Imam himself said: "انا النبأ العظیم" (I am the Great News) (50). Just as in Dua Nudbah, we address Imam Mahdi عليه السلام saying: "یابن النباء العظیم" (O son of the Great News). This meaning is more compatible with the pronoun "Huwa" (هو), which is used for humans, not objects. The intent of "al-Mala' al-A'la" (the Exalted Assembly) is the same as "al-Ufuq al-A'la" (the Highest Horizon) which we read in Surah An-Najm: "و هو بالافق الاعلى" (and He was in the highest horizon). The purpose of the Mi'raj (ascension) was to elevate the Prophet's level of knowledge: "لنریه من آیاتنا" (that We might show him some of Our signs). Therefore, perhaps the mentioned verses follow this point: O Prophet! Tell the people: Ali ibn Abi Talib is a great news from which some of you Muslims turn away (and go toward someone else). I was unaware of the higher realm when people disputed over his Wilayah (guardianship), but during the journey of Mi'raj, the Imamate of Ali ibn Abi Talib was presented and established. And do not think that his Imamate is my personal opinion; everything is Divine revelation. Of course, this meaning was derived with the aid of numerous narrations and the guidance of Tafsir Atayab al-Bayan, but other commentaries consider this verse an introduction to the subsequent verses regarding the creation of Adam.

50) كافى، ج ۸، ص ۳۰ .

Messages

1- The duty and position of the Prophet must be announced to the people. "قل" (Say).

2- Where heedlessness has encompassed everyone, one must speak from the perspective of warning (Inzar). "انّما أنا منذر" (I am only a warner).

3- Humanity faces dangers and harms from which it can be saved through the warnings of the Prophets. "أنا منذر" (I am a warner).

4- The role of warning (Inzar) in nurturing is greater than the role of glad tidings (Bisharat). "أنا منذر" (I am a warner).

5- Warning is effective when it originates from the source of power. "أنا منذر - الواحد القهّار" (I am a warner - The One, The Dominant).

6- Authority is achieved under the shadow of unity. "الواحد القهّار" (The One, The Dominant). (The word Qahhar appears six times in the Quran, and in all instances, it is paired with the word Wahid [One]).

7- The entire existence is under one management and will. "الواحد القهّار ربّ السموات والارض" (The One, The Dominant, Lord of the heavens and the earth).

8- Allah nurtures all of existence and guides everything toward perfection. "رب السموات والارض و ما بینهما" (Lord of the heavens and the earth and what is between them).

9- The Nurturer must possess both power and mercy. "ربّ... العزیز الغفّار" (Lord... The Mighty, The Forgiving).

10- The acceptance or rejection of the people is not a sign of the truth or falsehood of a matter. "هو نبؤ عظيم انتم عنه معرضون" (It is a great news from which you are turning away).

11- In propagating and guiding people, use clear and unambiguous methods. "نذیر مبین" (a clear warner).

Ayahs

إِذْ قَالَ رَبُّكَ لِلْمَلَـٰٓئِكَةِ إِنِّى خَـٰلِقٌۢ بَشَرًا مِّن طِينٍ (۷۱)
When your Lord said to the angels, ‘Indeed I am about to create a human being out of clay. (71)
فَإِذَا سَوَّيْتُهُۥ وَنَفَخْتُ فِيهِ مِن رُّوحِى فَقَعُوا۟ لَهُۥ سَـٰجِدِينَ (۷۲)
So when I have proportioned him and breathed into him of My spirit, then fall down in prostration before him.’ (72)
فَسَجَدَ ٱلْمَلَـٰٓئِكَةُ كُلُّهُمْ أَجْمَعُونَ (۷۳)
Thereat the angels prostrated, all of them together, (73)
إِلَّآ إِبْلِيسَ ٱسْتَكْبَرَ وَكَانَ مِنَ ٱلْكَـٰفِرِينَ (۷٤)
but not Iblis; he acted arrogantly and he was one of the faithless. (74)

Key Points

“Sawwaytuhu” (سوّیته) is derived from “Istiwā’” (استواء), meaning moderation and firmness/stability in creation. It is possible that the phrase “idh qāla” (اذ قال) relates to the phrase “takhāṣamūn” (تحیمون) in the previous verses, meaning, "I was not aware of the Exalted Assembly (al-Malā’ al-A‘lā) when the angels were disputing with Allah concerning the creation of man." As we read in Ayah 30 of Sūrat al-Baqarah, Allah said to the angels: "I am placing a vicegerent on earth." They replied: "Man is a corrupting and blood-shedding creature; disregard his creation, and we praise and glorify You with Your praise."

The story of the creation of man and the prostration of the angels to him is mentioned in Sūrahs al-Baqarah, al-A‘rāf, al-Ḥijr, al-Isrā’, and al-Kahf. The repetition of this story indicates the fanaticism and racism of Iblīs, his envy toward man, and the danger of arrogance, lack of repentance, and refusal to apologize—issues that all human beings struggle with throughout history in some way. On the other hand, it serves to draw man's attention to the dignity of humanity. A human being who is prostrated to by the angels must be vigilant not to squander his worth.

The meaning of “nafakhtu fīhi min rūḥī” (نفختُ فیه من روحى) is not that something separated from God and attached itself to man, but rather it is an explanation of the origin and root of the human soul, which is from the higher realm, not the earthly realm.

It is narrated from the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله that He said: Allah created Adam and placed us, the Ahl al-Bayt, in his progeny.
