Because of our dignity, He issued the command to prostrate. Thus, Adam being prostrated to was an act of servitude (worship) toward Allah, and an honor for Adam, because we were in his loins! (51)

(51) Tafsīr Nūr al-Thaqalayn.

Messages

1- Man is the only creature whose creation Allah announced to the angels before creating him. “Qāla Rabbuka” (قال ربّك)

2- The creation of the angels preceded that of man. (Because Allah spoke with the angels before [creating] man). “Qāla Rabbuka lil-malā’ikati” (قال ربّك للملائكة)

3- The origin of man's existence is water and earth (clay). “Khāliqun basharan min ṭīn” (خالقٌ بشراً من طین)

4- The spirit was created after the body. “Fa idhā sawwaytuhu wa nafakhktu fīhi min rūḥī” (فاذا سوّیته و نفخت فیه من روحى)

5- The spirit is independent and separate from the body. “Nafakhktu fīhi min rūḥī” (نفخت فیه من روحى)

6- The angels prostrated to Adam because of the Divine breath (of the spirit), yet some human beings do not prostrate to God even for the sake of the Divine Essence itself. “min rūḥī... lahu sājidīn” (من روحى... له ساجدین)

7- Man possesses two dimensions: material and spiritual. “min ṭīn - min rūḥī” (من طین - من روحى)

8- Divine favors require a receptive ground. Until the material capacities are addressed, the function of the Divine Spirit within it is not possible. “Fa idhā sawwaytuhu wa nafakhktu fīhi min rūḥī” (فاذا سوّیته و نفخت فیه من روحى)

9- The spirit exists in the bodies of all living creatures, but the expression “rūḥī” (My Spirit) is specific to man, who possesses a special honor. “Nafakhktu fīhi min rūḥī” (نفخت فیه من روحى)

10- The spirit is a subtle phenomenon. (The word nafkha (نفح) and 'blowing' indicate the subtlety of the spirit.)

11- Angels, like humans, are subject to command and prohibition. “Faqa‘ū lahu sājidīn” (فقعوا له ساجدین)

12- Prostration to man was due to his spiritual dimension, not his physical one. “Nafakhktu fīhi min rūḥī faqa‘ū” (نفخت فیه من روحى فقعوا)

13- Prostration to Adam, since it was by Allah's command, was servitude to Allah, not servitude to Adam. “Faqa‘ū lahu sājidīn” (فقعوا له ساجدین) (Prostration is a manifestation of honoring, dignifying, and accepting man's vicegerency, not worshipping him).

14- Merit is more important than seniority. (The angels had seniority over Adam, but because man's merit was greater, they prostrated to him.) “lahu sājidīn” (له ساجدین)

15- The angels are submissive to Allah. “Fa sajada al-malā’ikatu kulluhum” (فسجد الملائكة كلهم)

16- Collective worship is more magnificent. All the angels prostrated, “kulluhum” (كلّهم), and that too, collectively. “ajma‘ūn” (اجمعون)

17- Being among the good is not important; being one of the good is important. “Fa sajada al-malā’ikatu kulluhum - illā Iblīs” (فسجد الملائكة كلّهم - الاّ ابلیس)

18- Arrogance is an obstacle to devotion and submission. “Istakbara wa kāna...” (استكبر و كان...)

19- Iblīs was an unbeliever from the beginning, but his refusal to prostrate revealed his disbelief. “Kāna min al-kāfirīn” (كان من الكافرین)

Ayahs

قَالَ يَـٰٓإِبْلِيسُ مَا مَنَعَكَ أَن تَسْجُدَ لِمَا خَلَقْتُ بِيَدَىَّ ۖ أَسْتَكْبَرْتَ أَمْ كُنتَ مِنَ ٱلْعَالِينَ (۷٥)
He said, ‘O Iblis! What keeps you from prostrating before that which I have created with My [own] two hands? Are you arrogant, or are you one of the exalted ones?’ (75)
قَالَ أَنَا۠ خَيْرٌ مِّنْهُ ۖ خَلَقْتَنِى مِن نَّارٍ وَخَلَقْتَهُۥ مِن طِينٍ (۷٦)
‘I am better than him,’ he said. ‘You created me from fire and You created him from clay.’ (76)
قَالَ فَٱخْرُجْ مِنْهَا فَإِنَّكَ رَجِيمٌ (۷۷)
He said, ‘Begone hence, for you are indeed an outcast, (77)
وَإِنَّ عَلَيْكَ لَعْنَتِىٓ إِلَىٰ يَوْمِ ٱلدِّينِ (۷۸)
and indeed My curse will be on you till the Day of Retribution.’ (78)

Key Points

According to the Prophet of Islam صلى الله علیه وآله, the meaning of al-ʿālīn (the exalted ones) is a group that is superior to the angels and originates from a different station, stage, and place. (52) Perhaps the Prophet's intention is what we read in Ziyarat Jami'ah addressing the Ahl al-Bayt علیهم السلام: «خلقكم اللّه انوارا فجعلكم بعرشه محدقین حتّى من علینا بكم فجعلكم فى بیوت اذن اللّه ان یرفع و یذكر» (Allah created you as lights and placed you surrounding His Throne, until He favored us through you, placing you in houses which Allah permitted to be raised and remembered). This means Allah placed you as lights upon the Throne, and then, for humanity to access guidance, He placed you in regions of the earth to be the locus of the call to Monotheism. Wa Allāhu al-ʿĀlim (And Allah knows best).

According to Imam Sadiq علیه السلام, the meaning of the two hands mentioned in the Ayah is the hand of Power and Wisdom; otherwise, Allah is not a body to possess a physical hand. (53)

52) Tafsir Atayib al-Bayan. 53) Tafsir Atayib al-Bayan.

Messages

1- Demand an explanation from the transgressor and investigate the roots of the transgression. «يَا إِبْلِيسُ مَا مَنَعَكَ» (Give the offender a chance to speak).

2- The fundamental principle is inclination toward and acceptance of the truth; wherever you see the opposite, an impediment has arisen. «مَا مَنَعَكَ»

3- The creation of man was done with Allah's special attention. «خَلَقْتُ بِيَدَيَّ»

4- Ijtihād (personal reasoning) against a clear text (naṣṣ) is the work of Iblis. «فَاقْعُوا لَهُ سَاجِدِينَ... أَنَا خَيْرٌ»

5- Belief in Allah and His being the Creator is not sufficient; rather, obedience and submission to Him are necessary. (Iblis accepted Him as the Creator but lacked submission and obedience). «خَلَقْتَنِي»

6- Conjectural comparisons cannot be the source of correct evaluation. (Iblis compared himself to man and drew a conclusion). «أَنَا خَيْرٌ مِنْهُ - خَلَقْتَنِي...»

7- Racism is a Satanic ideology. «مِن نَّارٍ - مِن طِينٍ»

8- Fanaticism prevents the recognition of truth. (Iblis sees his fiery origin but fails to see the Divine breath in man). «خَلَقْتَنِي مِن نَّارٍ»

9- Honor lies in acquiring perfections, not in the primary matter or element, such that it is said man is from clay and Iblis is from fire. «مِن نَّارٍ - مِن طِينٍ... فَاخْرُجْ»

10- In management, the expulsion of an individual who rebels against the law is necessary. «فَاخْرُجْ مِنْهَا»

11- The result of arrogance and envy is deprivation. «فَاخْرُجْ»

12- Undesirable elements must be expelled from society. (The word rajīm (accursed/rejected) appears six times in the Quran, all referring to Iblis). «فَاخْرُجْ... إِنَّكَ رَجِيمٌ»

13- The humiliation of Satan was both spatial («فَاخْرُجْ») and positional (His curse/rejection). «لَعْنَتِي»

Ayahs

قَالَ رَبِّ فَأَنظِرْنِىٓ إِلَىٰ يَوْمِ يُبْعَثُونَ (۷۹)
He said, ‘My Lord! Respite me till the day they will be resurrected.’ (79)
قَالَ فَإِنَّكَ مِنَ ٱلْمُنظَرِينَ (۸٠)
Said He, ‘You are indeed among the reprieved (80)
إِلَىٰ يَوْمِ ٱلْوَقْتِ ٱلْمَعْلُومِ (۸۱)
until the day of the known time.’ (81)
قَالَ فَبِعِزَّتِكَ لَأُغْوِيَنَّهُمْ أَجْمَعِينَ (۸۲)
He said, ‘By Your might, I will surely pervert them, (82)
إِلَّا عِبَادَكَ مِنْهُمُ ٱلْمُخْلَصِينَ (۸۳)
except Your exclusive servants among them.’ (83)

Key Points

Imam Ali عليه السلام says: The reason Allah granted respite to Iblis was the filling of the measure and the completion of his trial (or test).(54)

54) Nahj al-Balaghah, Sermon 1.

Messages

1- Iblis did not ask for forgiveness due to arrogance; rather, he asked for respite in order to seek revenge. «انظرنى... اغوینّهم»

2- Despite all his arrogance and disbelief, Iblis did not despair from stating his request. «ربّ فانظرنى»

3- Life span is in the hand of Allah. «ربّ فانظرنى»

4- Saying «ربّ» (Rabb/Lord) in supplication is effective. (Both the friends of Allah and Iblis have used this word in their supplications). «ربّ فانظرنى»

5- Iblis knew about the Resurrection (Ma'ad) «الى یوم یبعثون», He knew about Monotheism (Tawhid) «ربّ», and He knew about Prophethood (Nubuwwah). «عبادك منهم المخلصین» Therefore, his problem was not ignorance of the fundamentals of religion, but rather arrogance and obstinacy.

6- Allah answers the supplication even of the worst of His creation. «ربّ فانظرنى... فانك من المنظرین»

7- Besides Iblis, some others also have a long lifespan. «من المنظرین»

8- Not every long lifespan is a sign of Allah's grace and affection or a factor for happiness. «من المنظرین»

9- It is the tradition (Sunnah) of Allah to make everyone taste death, and thus the long lifespan of Iblis was not guaranteed until the Day of Resurrection, but was granted until a specific day. «الى یوم الوقت المعلوم»

10- The danger of Iblis's temptations (waswasah) is serious. (He swore that he would mislead everyone in some way). «فبعزّتك لاغوینّهم»

11- Sometimes one sin becomes the prelude to greater sins. (The sin of not prostrating to Adam becomes a prelude to the sin of misleading people). «لاغوینّهم»

12- Sincerity (Ikhlas) in servitude is the condition for immunity from the traps of Iblis. «الاّ عبادك منهم المخلصین»

Ayahs

قَالَ فَٱلْحَقُّ وَٱلْحَقَّ أَقُولُ (۸٤)
Said He, ‘The truth is that—and I speak the truth— (84)
لَأَمْلَأَنَّ جَهَنَّمَ مِنكَ وَمِمَّن تَبِعَكَ مِنْهُمْ أَجْمَعِينَ (۸٥)
I will surely fill hell with you and all those who follow you.’ (85)
قُلْ مَآ أَسْـَٔلُكُمْ عَلَيْهِ مِنْ أَجْرٍ وَمَآ أَنَا۠ مِنَ ٱلْمُتَكَلِّفِينَ (۸٦)
Say, ‘I do not ask you any reward for it, and I am no impostor. (86)
إِنْ هُوَ إِلَّا ذِكْرٌ لِّلْعَـٰلَمِينَ (۸۷)
It is just a reminder for all the nations, (87)
وَلَتَعْلَمُنَّ نَبَأَهُۥ بَعْدَ حِينٍۭ (۸۸)
and you will surely learn its tidings in due time.’ (88)

Key Points

The relationship between the speaker and the speech is of four types: a) Both the speaker is false (bāṭil) and the speech is false. b) The speaker is upon falsehood, but He speaks the truth (ḥaqq). c) The speaker is upon truth, but His speech is false. d) Both the speaker is true (ḥaqq) and His word is true. Allah, both He Himself is the Truth and His speech is the Truth. «قال فالحقّ و الحقّ اقول»

At the beginning of this Sūrah, mention (dhikr) was made, «ص والقران ذى الذكر». At the end of the Sūrah, dhikr is also mentioned in this manner: «ان هو الا ذكر للعالمین»

The meaning of takalluf (imposition/affectation) which the Prophet denies for himself, is that "My words are clear and logical, and I do not impose anything upon you." «ما انا من المتكلّفين»

As we mentioned regarding Ayah 67 of this Sūrah: «قل هم نبأ عظيم», based on narrations, the meaning of the «نبأ عظيم» (Great News) is the news of the Wilāyah (Guardianship) of ‘Alī ibn Abī Ṭālib علیهما السلام. The final Ayah of the Sūrah, similar to that place, uses the pronoun huwa (He/It) and states: This news will become manifest after a time, and you will become aware of it. «ان هو الا ذكر للعالمين و لتعلمنّ نبأه بعد حين»

It is interesting that in other verses, the reward for the Prophet's mission is mentioned as the love (mawaddah) of the Ahl al-Bayt, and in these verses too, attention is drawn to this matter after denying any material reward for the mission.

Messages

1- Allah speaks nothing but the truth. (The precedence of the word al-Ḥaqq over aqūl indicates that He speaks nothing but the truth). «و الحقّ اقول»

2- In the face of the insistence of the people of falsehood, you too must speak with seriousness. Satan said: «لاغوينّهم...» (I will surely mislead them...), and Allah said: «لأملئنّ جهنّم» (I will surely fill Hell).

3- On the Day of Judgment, the leaders of disbelief and their followers will be gathered in one place. «جهنّم منك و ممّن تبعك»

4- Satan has a body, because the filling of Hell requires Iblīs to possess a body. «لأملئنّ»

5- The preacher must declare his lack of expectation to the people. «قل ما اسئلكم عليه من اجر»

6- The condition for success in propagation is having no expectations from the people. «قل ما اسئلكم»

7- The Prophets had not only no material expectations, but no expectations whatsoever from the people. «من اجر»

8- One of the conditions for success in propagation is the absence of takalluf (affectation/imposition). (The believer does not put himself or others to hardship in action, speech, or social interaction). «ما انا من المتكلّفين» Artificiality, affectation (takalluf), and pretense are disliked and condemned actions.

9- Only one who is not trapped by artificial constraints, habits, and customs can liberate the world. «ما انا من المتكلّفين ان هو الا ذكر للعالمين»

10- The mission of the Quran is universal and transnational. «ذكر للعالمين»

11- The Quran is a book of awakening and awareness. «ذكر للعالمين»

12- Without the Quran, humanity is exposed to various types of heedlessness (ghaflah) and forgetfulness (nisyān). «ان هو الا ذكر للعالمين»

13- Humanity will become aware in the future of the important news contained in the Quran (concerning the fulfillment of promises and the victory of the pious (muttaqīn) and the righteous (ṣāliḥīn)). «لتعلمنّ نبأه بعد حين»
